---
title: "Assemble plant species list for Barro Colorado Island and Barro Colorado Natural Monument, Panama"
author: "Paula Uzcategui and Helene Muller-Landau"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Summary

This Rmarkdown merges information from multiple high-quality plant species lists for Barro Colorado Island (BCI) and other Barro Colorado Natural Monument sites in Panama, updates to the best current woody plant species names based on the current Smithsonian ForestGeo woody species lists, utilizes prior checks of species names against the World Checklist of Vascular Plants (WCVP) version 13, and outputs the combined high-quality list. The reason for using WCVP version 13 is because that is the version currently in use by PlantNet.  The contributing lists are Garwood et al. 2009, Schnitzer et al. 2024, Zotz 2024, the Smithsonian ForestGeo Panama woody plant species and ViewTaxonomy lists (Perez et al. 2025), and a file of tree species found in forest inventory plots on BCI.  These lists are considered high-quality because they were each assembled by one or more botanists familiar with the local flora, and build on the work of Croat 1978. Combining the lists involves checking overlap not only in the original names on each list, but also on two lists of "synonyms" for BCI plant species, as well as in the current accepted name.  The synonym lists include one from Garwood 2009 and two from Smithsonian ForestGeo.  In all cases, these include not only synonyms in the botanical sense, but also prior names used for the same BCI morphospecies (including in cases where species determination was revised).  

This Rmarkdown additionally outputs a list of plant species that appear in panamabiota.org's list of plants of Barro Colorado, that are *not* in the combined high-quality list. This list is generated for the purpose of later review.  

The component and output lists are all available in the public GitHub site https://github.com/PanamaForestGEO/Panama_plant_species_lists 


## References 

Croat, Thomas B. 1978. *Flora of Barro Colorado Island*. Stanford University Press.

Garwood, Nancy C., Nancy Hensold, and Robin B. Foster. 2009. Appendix 1.  Revised Species List for Barro Colorado Island, with Synonyms. In *Seedlings of Barro Colorado Island and the Neotropics*. Cornell University Press.

Pérez, Rolando, Salomón Aguilar and David Mitre.  2025. Lista de árboles, arbustos, palmas, lianas y enredaderas de la flora de Panamá.  Version 2025-12-04.  

Schnitzer, Stefan, David DeFilippis, Rigoberto Rivera-Camaña, et al. 2024. Liana Species of the Barro Colorado Island 50-Ha Plot 2007 and 2017 Censuses. Version 7. *Dryad* https://doi.org/10.5061/DRYAD.Q2BVQ83V4.

Zotz, Gerhard.  2024.  Table S1. Updated list of epiphyte species on BCI. Online Supplementary Material for Zotz, G. 2024. Epiphyte Research on BCI. In *The First 100 Years of Research on Barro Colorado: Plant and Ecosystem Science*, ed. H. C. Muller-Landau and S. J. Wright. Smithsonian Institution Scholarly Press. https://doi.org/10.5479/si.26880865
    
## Setup

### Load libraries
```{r setup2,}

rm(list=ls())

library(readxl)
library(writexl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringi)
library(stringr)
library(knitr)
library(DT)
library(rgbif)
#install.packages("rgbif")
```

### Read input files and initial data prep

```{r readprepdata, echo=TRUE, results="hide"}
# --- File paths ---
FILE_GARWOOD          <- "splists_mid/species_list_WCVP/Garwood_2009_Appendix1HM2_WCVP13_2026-04-24.xlsx"
FILE_GARWOOD_SYN      <- "splists_raw/Garwood/Garwood_2009_Appendix1_Synonyms.xlsx"
FILE_ZOTZ             <- "splists_mid/species_list_WCVP/83_Zotz_Epiphytes_TableS1_WCVP13_2026-04-24.xlsx"
FILE_LIANAS           <- "splists_mid/species_list_WCVP/Lianas_Species_complete_list_WCVP13_2026-03-16.xlsx"
FILE_PBIOTA           <- "splists_mid/species_list_WCVP/PanamaBiotaBCI_WCVP13_2026-02-26.xlsx"
FILE_PANWOODY         <- "splists_mid/species_list_WCVP/CurrentPanamaWoody_2026-03-26.xlsx"
FILE_PANWOODY_SYN     <- "splists_mid/alternates_and_synonyms/CurrentPanamaWoody_SYNONYMS_2026-03-26.xlsx"
FILE_ALTNAMES         <- "splists_mid/alternates_and_synonyms/AltNames_2026-03-31.xlsx"
FILE_ALTNAMES_GARWOOD <- "splists_mid/alternates_and_synonyms/alternate_names_garwood_2026-03-31.xlsx"
FILE_WRIGHT           <- "splists_mid/species_list_WCVP/wright_nomenclature_R_20210224_WCVP13_2026-04-08.xlsx"

# --- Load data ---

# Original Garwood list + WCVP v13 names
garwood_list_orig <- read_excel(FILE_GARWOOD, col_types = "text")

# Garwood synonyms
garwood_synonyms <- read_excel(FILE_GARWOOD_SYN)

# Original Zotz list + WCVP v13 names
zotz_list <- read_excel(FILE_ZOTZ, col_types = "text") %>%
  rename_with(~ gsub("[()]", "", gsub(" ", "_", .x))) %>%
  rename(wcvp_accepted_lifeform = wcvp_lifeform_description)

# Original lianas list + WCVP v13 names
lianas_list <- read_excel(FILE_LIANAS, col_types = "text") %>%
  mutate(sp6 = tolower(sp6)) %>%
  rename(wcvp_accepted_lifeform = wcvp_lifeform_description)

# Original Panama biota list + WCVP v13 names
pbiota_list <- read_excel(FILE_PBIOTA, col_types = "text")

# Current Panama woody species list + WCVP v13 names
# This list has the names used in ForestGeo in the column "orig_name"
panwoody_list <- read_excel(FILE_PANWOODY, col_types = "text")

#changing one column to make it match easier
panwoody_list <- panwoody_list %>% rename(
  orig_author = orig_authority
)

panwoody_synonyms <- read_excel(FILE_PANWOODY_SYN, col_types = "text")

# Alternate names of species in Garwood and Panama woody
alternate_names <- read_excel(FILE_ALTNAMES)

# Alternate names of species in Garwood and Panama woody that are directly or indirectly in Garwood
alternate_names_garwood <- read_excel(FILE_ALTNAMES_GARWOOD)

# Wright list
allwrighttaxa <- read_excel(FILE_WRIGHT, na = c("", "NA"))
```

Adding some columns to the Garwood list
```{r prepare garwood}
garwood_synonyms <- garwood_synonyms %>% mutate(
  sp_name = trimws(paste0(Genus, " ", Species)),
  synonym_sp = trimws(paste0(SynonymGenusSingle, " ", SynonymSpeciesSingle)),
  synonym_binomial = paste0(SynonymGenusSingle, " ", SynonymSpeciesSingle)) 
# note that synonym_sp and synonym_binomial are identical; retaining for clarity of completeness

garwood_list <- garwood_list_orig %>%
  mutate(wcvp_accepted_binomial = 
           paste0(wcvp_accepted_name_genus, " ", wcvp_accepted_name_species),
         wcvp_matched_binomial = word(wcvp_matched_name,)) %>%
  rename(wcvp_accepted_lifeform = wcvp_lifeform_description)
```


*alternate_names_garwood* is a list of alternate names that match to the Garwood list. It is generated in the *alternate_species_names.Rmd* to be used in this script.

```{r buildaltmatchlookup}
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Build a flat lookup table used downstream to resolve BCI plot species and
# lianas list entries that did not match Garwood directly.
# Both the preferred name and the alternate name are indexed as possible keys
# so that either form can be used to retrieve the Garwood match.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

alt_match_lookup <- bind_rows(
  alternate_names_garwood %>%
    select(match_key = prefname, garwood_orig_match, garwood_synonym_match,
           firstmatchtype, alt_name = altname, pref_name = prefname),
  alternate_names_garwood %>%
    select(match_key = altname,  garwood_orig_match, garwood_synonym_match,
           firstmatchtype, alt_name = altname, pref_name = prefname)
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  distinct(match_key, .keep_all = TRUE)



```


### Output file names 

MAIN OUTPUTS: 

- Combined, high-quality list of BCNM plant species.  

- List of BCI species that might be woody according to WCVP lifeform. 

ADDITIONAL OUTPUTS OF FILES TO CHECK: 

- List of Zotz species that are not in Garwood or conflicting with a species in Garwood. 

- List of BCI plot species that need revision because they are not in Garwood or conflicting with a species in Garwood

- List of BCI liana list species that need revision because they are not in Garwood or conflicting with a species in Garwood.

- List of Wright species that are not in the Current Panama Woody list but that might be woody according to the lifeform in Wright, WCVP and Garwood. 

- List of PanamaBiota BCI species that are not in the final high-quality list


```{r outfilenames}

current_date <- Sys.Date()

FILECOMBINEDLIST <- paste0("splists_out/BCNM_SPECIES_BOTANISTS_LIST_",current_date,".xlsx")

FILEBCIWOODY <- paste0("tocheck/BCNM_SPECIES_POSSIBLY_WOODY_",current_date,".xlsx")

FILECHECKPLOTS <- paste0("tocheck/plotsbci_not_garwood_", current_date, ".xlsx")

FILECHECKLIANAS <- paste0("tocheck/lianasbci_not_garwood_", current_date, ".xlsx")

FILECHECKPANAMABIOTA <- paste0("tocheck/panamabiota_not_garwood_", current_date, ".xlsx")

FILECHECKWRIGHT <- paste0("tocheck/Wright_not_CurrentPanamaWoody_", current_date, ".xlsx")

FILECHECKZOTZ <- paste0("tocheck/zotz_garwood_check_", current_date, ".xlsx")
```

## Assembling a combined BCI high-quality botanist-determined plant species list

```{r columnsetup}
# Initializing the columns that all of the species added should have
listscol <- c("orig_name",
              "orig_binomial",
              "orig_author",
              "wcvp_accepted_binomial",
              "wcvp_matched_name",
              "wcvp_matched_authors",
              "wcvp_matched_plant_name_id",
              "wcvp_matched_ipni_id",
              "wcvp_matched_status",
              "match_similarity",
              "wcvp_accepted_name",
              "wcvp_accepted_family", 
              "wcvp_accepted_authority",
              "wcvp_accepted_plant_name_id", 
              "wcvp_accepted_powo_id",
              "wcvp_accepted_ipni_id",
              "wcvp_accepted_lifeform",
              "source_list"
              )

# Create the empty dataframe of BCI species list
bcilist <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = length(listscol), nrow = 0))
colnames(bcilist) <- listscol

```


### Garwood 2009 list

Some species in Garwood are listed as being only in the Barro Colorado Nature Monument but not on BCI itself (chg=="BCNM"). However, after matching with species recorded in the plots in BCI these species show up:

Matayba oppositifolia, Myrcia zetekiana, Buchenavia tetraphylla. 

(Buchenavia tetraphylla is an alternate name of Terminalia tetraphylla which is found in the plot censuees of BCI)


```{r garwoodnobcnm}

yesonbcibinomial <- c("Matayba oppositifolia", "Myrcia zetekiana", "Buchenavia tetraphylla")

garwood_list <- garwood_list %>% mutate(
  garwood_BCNM = ifelse(!is.na(chg) & chg == "BCNM", TRUE, FALSE)
) 

garwoodBCNM <- subset(garwood_list_orig, chg=="BCNM")

kable(garwoodBCNM %>% select(orig_name,SYNONYMS),
      caption = "Species that according to Garwood are in the mainland peninsulas but not on BCI itself.")
```

Removing some conflicting species from the Garwood list:

- NA names

- Ficus aff. Trigonata, it is already in zotz without the aff

- Citrus x aurantium NA (Sour Orange Group) which has the same name as Citrus x aurantium NA (Sweet Orange Group)

- Aspidosperma excelsum, this is the name in Garwood but the STRI botanists agree that Aspidosperma Megalocarpon, is the species in BCNM

- Nectandra umbrosa, this is actually Damburneya purpurea which is in the plots census

- Piper culebranum, this is actually  Piper colonense which is in the plots census

- Quararibea asterolepis, this is actually Quararibea stenophylla which is in the plots census.

```{r garwoodmod}
species_remove_garwood <- c(
"Ficus aff. trigonata",  "Citrus x aurantium NA (Sour Orange Group)",
"Aspidosperma excelsum", "Nectandra umbrosa",
"Piper culebranum", "Quararibea asterolepis"
)

garwood_list <- garwood_list %>% filter(!is.na(orig_name)  & !orig_name %in% species_remove_garwood)
garwood_synonyms <- garwood_synonyms %>% filter(!sp_name %in% species_remove_garwood)
alt_match_lookup <- alt_match_lookup %>% filter(!garwood_orig_match %in% species_remove_garwood)

# Add this species to garwood instead of  "Aspidosperma excelsum"

megalocarpon <- panwoody_list %>% 
  filter(orig_name == "Aspidosperma megalocarpon") %>% 
  mutate(source_list = "Garwood2009",
         garwood_BCNM = FALSE) %>% 
  select(all_of(listscol))


garwood_list <- bind_rows(garwood_list,
          megalocarpon)

```


```{r garwoodnoid}

# view unidentified species
garwoodnobinomial <- subset(garwood_list_orig,is.na(word(orig_binomial,2)))

kable(garwoodnobinomial %>% select(orig_name,Syn_notes) %>% rename(garwood_name=orig_name),
      caption="Garwood species lacking a complete binomial")

```

In `r nrow(garwoodnobinomial)` cases these species lack proper binomials, indicating their species identity remains uncertain (including possible new species).  These are left in anyway because they are known morphospecies.  
 
We have `r nrow(garwood_list)` plant species on the Garwood list. 

Add Garwood species to the BCI list. 
```{r startbcilist}

garwood_list$source_list = "Garwood2009"
garwood_list$in_garwood = TRUE
garwood_list$garwood_name = garwood_list$orig_name
garwood_list$garwood_synonyms = garwood_list$SYNONYMS

          
# Some additional columns in Garwood. 
bcilist <- garwood_list %>% select(all_of(c(listscol,"in_garwood","garwood_name", "garwood_synonyms", "LF_RF", "BCI cult", "garwood_BCNM")))

bcilist <- bcilist %>% mutate(
  bci_rowid = row_number()
)
```  

Create lookup table
```{r garwood}

# garwood look up table
garwood_synonym_full <- garwood_synonyms %>%
  select(garwood_name= sp_name, garwood_syn = synonym_sp) %>%
  # Bring in the full data for the accepted garwood_name
  inner_join(garwood_list %>% 
               select(garwood_name = orig_name, 
                      garwood_wcvp_accepted = wcvp_accepted_name,
                      garwood_synonym = SYNONYMS
                      ), 
             by = "garwood_name")%>%
  distinct() # Ensure no duplicate mapping rows

garwood_list$garwood_name <- garwood_list$orig_name
  
garwood_list_lookup <- bind_rows(
  garwood_list %>% select(match_key = orig_name, garwood_name, garwood_wcvp_accepted = wcvp_accepted_name, garwood_synonym=  SYNONYMS), 
  garwood_list %>% select(match_key = orig_binomial, garwood_name, garwood_wcvp_accepted = wcvp_accepted_name, garwood_synonym= SYNONYMS), 
  garwood_list %>% select(match_key = wcvp_matched_binomial, garwood_name, garwood_wcvp_accepted = wcvp_accepted_name,  garwood_synonym= SYNONYMS), 
  garwood_list %>% select(match_key = wcvp_matched_name, garwood_name, garwood_wcvp_accepted = wcvp_accepted_name, garwood_synonym=  SYNONYMS), 
  garwood_list %>% select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_name, garwood_name, garwood_wcvp_accepted = wcvp_accepted_name,  garwood_synonym= SYNONYMS), 
  garwood_synonym_full %>% select(match_key = garwood_syn, garwood_name, garwood_wcvp_accepted, garwood_synonym), 
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  distinct(match_key, .keep_all = TRUE)


```

---


### Zotz 2024 epiphyte species list

*There are `r nrow(zotz_list)` species on the Zotz 2024 epiphyte list.  *

```{r prepzotz}

zotz_list <- zotz_list %>% mutate(
  wcvp_matched_binomial = word(wcvp_matched_name,1,2),
) 

# setting column indicating zotz list presence
zotz_list$source_list = "Zotz"
zotz_list$zotz_name = zotz_list$orig_name


# removing morphospecies identified only at the genus level (there is only one)
zotz_list <- zotz_list %>% 
  filter(is.na(orig_binomial) | !is.na(word(orig_binomial,2) )) 


# when the name listed is the same as in Croat 1978, the column Sp_Croat_1978 is blank (NA)
# so create a new name_Croat field that is the name of the species in Croat 1978
zotz_list <- zotz_list %>% 
  mutate(wcvp_accepted_binomial = 
           paste0(wcvp_accepted_name_genus," ",wcvp_accepted_name_species),
         wcvp_matched_binomial = word(wcvp_matched_name,1,2),
         croat_name = ifelse(!is.na(Sp_Croat_1978),Sp_Croat_1978,orig_name)) %>%
  mutate(croat_binomial = word(croat_name,1,2)) 

# make one manual correction so matches work right
zotz_list <- zotz_list %>%
  mutate(croat_name =ifelse(croat_name=="Bolbiltis nicotianifolia",
         "Bolbitis nicotianifolia",croat_name))
```

Next, we manually remove some species from the Zotz list that were not identified correctly.
```{r removezotz}

# Some species in Zotz are not correct, here we manually remove them from the list to not add incorrect species

zotz_incorrect_species <- c("Ficus curtipes")

zotz_list <- zotz_list %>% filter(!orig_name %in% zotz_incorrect_species)

```


#### 1. Direct match to garwood names

```{r zotzdirect}
# 1. direct name match
bcilist <- bcilist %>% 
  left_join(zotz_list %>% select(zotz_name, orig_name), by="orig_name")


# Match by wcvp matched name (the species name is slightly different)
bcilist <- bcilist %>% 
  left_join(zotz_list %>% select(zozt_name_matchwcvp = zotz_name, wcvp_matched_name), by="wcvp_matched_name") %>%
  mutate(
    zotz_name = coalesce(zotz_name, zozt_name_matchwcvp)
  )

datatable(
  bcilist %>%
    filter(!is.na(zotz_name))%>%
    select(garwood_name, zotz_name, wcvp_matched_name), 
          caption="Zotz species matching directly to Garwood names",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))  

# Set the Zotz name (the spelling is Zotz as the original name.)
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  mutate(
    orig_name = coalesce(zotz_name, orig_name),
    source_list = ifelse(!is.na(zotz_name), "Zotz", source_list)
  )

zotz_garwood_name_match <- bcilist %>% filter(!is.na(zotz_name))
```
          
*Matched `r nrow(zotz_garwood_name_match)` species in the plots directly to garwood.*

#### 2. Indirect name match 
```{r zotzalter}


# 2. everything that did not match directly to garwood, match to alternate names
zotz_garwood_indirect_match <- zotz_list %>% 
  filter(
      !zotz_name %in% bcilist$zotz_name &
    (
      orig_name %in% garwood_list_lookup$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% garwood_list_lookup$match_key |
        
      (!is.na(croat_name) &
         croat_name %in% garwood_list_lookup$match_key) |
      
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_binomial) &
         wcvp_matched_binomial %in% garwood_list_lookup$match_key) |
        
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
         wcvp_matched_name %in% garwood_list_lookup$match_key) |
        
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
         wcvp_accepted_name %in% garwood_list_lookup$match_key)
    )
  )


# 3. print the altname it matched to and why that name is in garwood

zotz_garwood_indirect_match_print <- zotz_garwood_indirect_match %>%
  # Join using a chain of coalesce to check the most reliable fields first
  left_join(garwood_list_lookup, by = c("orig_name" = "match_key")) %>%
  left_join(garwood_list_lookup, by = c("orig_binomial" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_bi")) %>%
  

  left_join(garwood_list_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_name" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_match")) %>%
  left_join(garwood_list_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_binomial" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_matchbi")) %>%
  
    left_join(garwood_list_lookup, by = c("croat_name" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_croat")) %>%
  
  left_join(garwood_list_lookup, by = c("wcvp_accepted_name" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_acc")) %>%
  
  # Now, condense everything into final fields
  mutate(
    garwood_name    = coalesce(
      garwood_name,
      garwood_name_bi,
      garwood_name_match,
      garwood_name_matchbi,
      garwood_name_croat,
      garwood_name_acc),
    
    garwood_wcvp_accepted    = coalesce(
      garwood_wcvp_accepted,
      garwood_wcvp_accepted_bi,
      garwood_wcvp_accepted_match,
      garwood_wcvp_accepted_matchbi,
      garwood_wcvp_accepted_croat,
      garwood_wcvp_accepted_acc),
    
    garwood_synonym = coalesce(
      garwood_synonym,
      garwood_synonym_bi,
      garwood_synonym_match, 
      garwood_synonym_matchbi,
      garwood_synonym_croat,
      garwood_synonym_acc),
    
  ) %>%
  select(-ends_with("_bi"), -ends_with("_match"), -ends_with("_matchbi"), 
         -ends_with("_croat"),-ends_with("_acc")) # Clean up extra columns

```

*Matched `r nrow(zotz_garwood_indirect_match)` species in Zotz by synonyms or alternate names to garwood.*


```{r zotzprintaltmatches}
datatable(
  zotz_garwood_indirect_match_print %>% 
  select(zotz_name, croat_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, garwood_name, garwood_synonym), 
          caption="Zotz species that match indirectly to Garwood",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))
```

#### Changing Garwood to Zotz name, since the Zotz name is better in some cases.

Set the current name to the Zotz name if:

1. The WCVP accepted name of the Garwood name is the same as the current Zotz name.

2. The Garwood name is the same as the Croat name 

3. Zotz and Garwood have the same binomial, And the match is one-one. 

4. Zotz and Garwood have the same binomial, but there is more than one match, keep the one were the subspecies is the same as the species. 

```{r garwoodchangezotz}

#1. Change because Zotz is more up-to-date 
zotz_wcvp_change <- zotz_garwood_indirect_match_print %>% 
  filter(
  garwood_wcvp_accepted == wcvp_matched_name |
  garwood_name == croat_name 
  )

datatable(
  zotz_wcvp_change %>%
  select( garwood_name, garwood_wcvp_accepted, garwood_synonym, zotz_name, croat_name,wcvp_accepted_name), 
          caption="Garwood names should change to the Zotz names since they are more up-to-date",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


# Make sure that the names that will be changed don't generate a conflict: 
datatable(garwood_list %>% filter(
  garwood_name %in% zotz_wcvp_change$zotz_name
) %>% select(garwood_name)
, caption="Zotz name is already in Garwood")


#3. Change because its the same species, but one list is using subspecies
zotz_disctinct_subp <- zotz_garwood_indirect_match_print %>% 
  filter(!zotz_name %in% zotz_wcvp_change$zotz_name & 
           # make sure they have subspecies
          (!is.na(word(zotz_name,3)) | !is.na(word(garwood_name, 3))) & 
           # see that they matched by the binomial
           (orig_binomial %in% garwood_list_lookup$match_key |
          (!is.na(wcvp_matched_binomial) &
          wcvp_matched_binomial %in% garwood_list_lookup$match_key))) %>%
          add_count(garwood_name, name="n_garwood") %>%
          add_count(zotz_name, name = "n_zotz")%>%
    filter(n_zotz == 1 & n_garwood == 1) 


datatable(
  zotz_disctinct_subp %>%
  select( garwood_name, garwood_wcvp_accepted, garwood_synonym, zotz_name, croat_name,wcvp_accepted_name), 
          caption="Garwood names should change to the Zotz names, because they just have different subspecies",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)) 


# Make sure that the names that will be changed don't generate a conflict: 
datatable(garwood_list %>% filter(
  garwood_name %in% zotz_disctinct_subp$zotz_name
) %>% select(garwood_name)
, caption="Zotz name is already in Garwood")


  
```

The other species that are an indirect match but need revision: 
```{r zotzforrevision}

zotz_needs_check <- zotz_garwood_indirect_match_print %>% 
           filter(!zotz_name %in% zotz_wcvp_change$zotz_name &
                   !zotz_name %in% zotz_disctinct_subp$zotz_name
                    ) %>%
    select(zotz_name, croat_name, wcvp_accepted_name, garwood_name, garwood_wcvp_accepted, garwood_synonym)

datatable(
  zotz_needs_check,
  caption="Zotz species that need revision for their match to Garwood",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
)


```


```{r change_garwood_to_zotz_name}
zotz_garwood_change_name = bind_rows(zotz_wcvp_change,
                                     zotz_disctinct_subp)

bcilist_stay <- bcilist %>% 
  filter(!garwood_name %in% zotz_garwood_change_name$garwood_name)

bcilist_to_update <- bcilist %>% 
  filter(garwood_name %in% zotz_garwood_change_name$garwood_name) %>%
  select(-all_of(listscol), -any_of("zotz_name")) %>%
  left_join(zotz_garwood_change_name %>% 
              select(garwood_name, zotz_name, all_of(listscol)), 
            by = "garwood_name")

bcilist <- bind_rows(bcilist_stay, bcilist_to_update)
```


Printing species in Zotz that are  definitely not in Garwood. 
```{r allnomatchzotz}

# combine direct matches and matches by alternate names or synonyms
zotz_garwood_match <- bind_rows(
  zotz_garwood_name_match,
  zotz_garwood_indirect_match
) %>%
  distinct(zotz_name, .keep_all = TRUE)

# filter unmatched rows
zotz_no_garwood_at_all <- zotz_list %>%
  filter(!zotz_name %in% zotz_garwood_match$zotz_name) 

datatable(zotz_no_garwood_at_all%>% 
  select(zotz_name , wcvp_matched_name,wcvp_accepted_name, croat_name), 
          caption="Species in Zotz that don't match anything in Garwood")


# Writing file to be reviewed by a botanists
write_xlsx(
  bind_rows(zotz_needs_check,
            zotz_no_garwood_at_all),
            FILECHECKZOTZ)
```

```{r addzotzbci}

# Adding zotz species to the BCI list

bcilist <- bind_rows(
  bcilist,
  zotz_no_garwood_at_all %>% select(all_of(listscol), zotz_name))

bcilist <- bcilist %>% mutate(
  bci_rowid = row_number()
)

```


In total there are `r nrow(zotz_no_garwood_at_all)` species on the Zotz list not already on the Garwood list.  

---

Next we match species recorded in BCI and BCNM in plot census and in Lianas lists. Many of these species are expected to be in the Forestgeo list, in that case the Forestgeo name is more up-to-date than the Garwood name. We will first detect the species that are definitely missing from the BCNM list arranged so far and add them, and then we will find the Forestgeo names for all of the species in the final list. 


### List of tree species recorded in censuses of forest plots on BCI 

Merging species from three sources of plot censuses: 

```{r plotslist}
# Preparing plotlist

# Plot abundance in BCNM
bci_plots_abund <- read_excel("splists_raw/Plots/abundance_plots_HM.xlsx")

bci_plots_abund <- bci_plots_abund %>% 
  select(sp, binomial, 
         # plots in bci
         bci, `bci 10 ha plot`, P11, P14,
         
         # plots in BCNM
         P10, P12, P13, P18, gigante1, gigante2
         
         ) %>%
  rename(
    sp6 = sp,
    census_name = binomial,
    bci50ha = bci,
    bci10ha = `bci 10 ha plot`,
  ) 

# Other BCI census
bci_other_plots <- read.delim(
  "splists_raw/Plots/doi_10_5061_dryad_1g1jwsvc3__v20260226/mainfile.txt"
)

# summarise
bci_other_plots <- bci_other_plots %>%
  group_by(sp6, plot) %>%
  summarise(n_distinct_tag = n_distinct(tag), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = plot,
    values_from = n_distinct_tag,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  rename(
    bci10ha = `10ha`,
    bci25ha = `25ha`
  )

# Census from Gigante project
census_gigante <- read.csv(
  "splists_raw/Plots/gigante_census_2026-04-05.csv"
)
census_gigante <- census_gigante%>%select(sp6, freq) %>%
  rename(gigante_plot = freq)


# Join safely (keeps all census_list species)
census_list <- bci_plots_abund %>%
  full_join(bci_other_plots, by = "sp6") %>%
  mutate(bci10ha = coalesce(bci10ha.x, bci10ha.y)) %>%
  select(-bci10ha.x, -bci10ha.y) %>%
  full_join(census_gigante, by = "sp6")

# Filter safely (handle NA just in case)
census_list <- census_list %>%
  mutate(across(c(bci50ha, bci10ha, P14, P11, Zetek, bci25ha, AVA, Drayton, Pearson, P10, P12, P13, P18, gigante1, gigante2, gigante_plot), ~coalesce(., 0))) %>%
  filter(
      if_any(c(P11, P14, bci10ha, bci50ha, Zetek, bci25ha, AVA, Drayton, Pearson, P10, P12, P13, P18, gigante1, gigante2, gigante_plot),
             ~ !is.na(.) & . > 0)
    )


census_list <- census_list %>% mutate(
  BCI = (bci50ha>0 | bci10ha>0 | P14>0 | P11>0 | Zetek>0 | bci25ha>0 | 
           AVA>0 | Drayton>0 | Pearson>0),
  
  # if it is in any of the plots outside of BCI that are in the BCNM
  Other_BCNM_plots = (P10 >0 | P12>0| P13>0| P18>0| gigante1>0| gigante2>0 | gigante_plot > 0)
)

# 6. Create census_plot column safely
census_list <- census_list %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    census_plot = paste(
      c(
        if (bci50ha > 0) "bci50ha",
        if (bci10ha > 0) "bci10ha",
        if(bci25ha > 0) "bci25ha",
        if (P14 > 0) "P14",
        if (P11 > 0) "P11",
        if(Zetek > 0) "Zetek",
        if(AVA > 0) "AVA",
        if(Drayton > 0) "Drayton",
        if(Pearson > 0) "Pearson",
        if (P10 > 0) "P10",
        if (P12 > 0) "P12",
        if (P13 > 0) "P13",
        if (P18 > 0) "P18",
        if (gigante1 > 0) "gigante1",
        if (gigante2 > 0) "gigante2",
        if (gigante_plot > 0) "gigante_plot"
      ),
      collapse = ", "
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# the plots files don't have wcvp accepted name, merge them by 6-letter species code in the Panama woody species list
match21 <- match(census_list$sp6, panwoody_list$sp6)            
nomatchview <- census_list[is.na(match21),] %>% select(sp6, census_name, census_plot) # Those that don't match are all unidentified

if (nrow(nomatchview)>0){
knitr::kable(nomatchview, caption="Warning: Species recorded in plots on BCI whose 6-letter codes do not have a match in the Panama ForestGeo woody plant species database.")

}else{
  print("All species in the plot have a match to a sp6 code in the current woody list.")
}

census_list <- census_list %>% left_join(
  panwoody_list, by="sp6"
) %>%
  rename(forestgeo_current_name = orig_name)%>% 
# leaving out species that dont have a name
  filter(!is.na(forestgeo_current_name))
  

# Some names in the plots do not match the names in forestgeo
datatable(
census_list %>% 
  filter(!is.na(census_name) & (census_name != forestgeo_current_name)) %>% 
  select(sp6, sp4, census_name, forestgeo_current_name = forestgeo_current_name),
caption="The name used in the plot for this species does not match the forestgeo name associated with the sp6 code used. Using the name associated with the sp6 code in forestgeo. "
)

datatable(
census_list %>% 
  filter(is.na(census_name)) %>% 
  select(sp6, sp4, census_name, forestgeo_current_name = forestgeo_current_name),
caption="Sp6 codes in the plots match to forestgeo codes, but species name is missing in the plots list. "
)

# We will use the forestgeo current name to make all the comparisons with this list
census_list <- census_list %>% mutate(orig_name = forestgeo_current_name)

# remove the row for "unidentified", if present
census_list <- census_list %>% filter(sp6!="uniden")

bcidups <- census_list %>% filter(duplicated(sp6)) %>% select(sp6, census_name, forestgeo_current_name)

if (nrow(bcidups)>0)
knitr::kable(bcidups, caption="Warning: Some 6-letter species codes are repeated in the census_list data.frame.")

# create binomial first if not already
census_list$wcvp_accepted_binomial <- paste0(
  census_list$wcvp_accepted_genus, " ",
  census_list$wcvp_accepted_species
)


census_list <- census_list %>% 
  mutate(
    in_census = TRUE,
    source_list = "Plots_census",
    census_row_id = row_number(),
    forestgeo_current_binomial = orig_binomial,
  )


datatable(
census_list %>%
  select(sp6, sp4, census_name, forestgeo_current_name, census_plot,BCI, Other_BCNM_plots),
caption="BCNM species recorded in plot census",
options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
)


species_only_bcnm <- census_list %>% filter(!BCI)
datatable(species_only_bcnm %>% select(sp6, forestgeo_current_name, census_plot),
          caption="Species that have been recorded in plots in BCNM but not in BCI")


species_only_bci <- census_list %>% filter(!Other_BCNM_plots)
datatable(species_only_bci %>% select(sp6, forestgeo_current_name, census_plot),
          caption="Species that have been recorded in BCI but not in the other plots in BCNM")

```


Some of the species in the census were not identified correctly:

After revision with the STRI botanists (2026-04-21):

- Guarea glabra, Miconia poeppigii, Heisteria costaricensis: these are older names from misidentification, looks like the codes were not updated while doing the census. They are actually: 
Guarea guidonia, Miconia minutiflora and Heisteria macrophylla. 

After verification on the field (2026-04-27): 

- Jupunba idiopoda, Myrciaria floribunda, Unonopsis panamensis were also misidentifications. The correct names are: Jupunba barbouriana, Calycolpus warszewiczianus, Unonopsis pittieri, which are already in the list.  


```{r remove_census_species}

species_remove_census <- c("Guarea glabra", "Miconia poeppigii", "Heisteria costaricensis","Jupunba idiopoda","Myrciaria floribunda","Unonopsis panamensis")

# These species are currently under revision in the field but wont be left out for now. 
species_remove_census2 <- 
  c(
    "Blighia sapida",
    "Coccoloba padiformis",
    "Croton schiedeanus",
    "Geonoma congesta",
    "Herrania nycterodendron",
    "Hevea brasiliensis",
    "Ixora miquelii",
    "Jupunba idiopoda",
    "Miconia simplex",
    "Myrciaria floribunda",
    "Protium trifoliolatum",
    "Unonopsis panamensis",
    "Virola elongata")


census_list <- census_list %>% filter(!forestgeo_current_name %in% species_remove_census)
                                      #&!forestgeo_current_name %in% species_remove_census2)

```


**In total there are `r nrow(census_list)` tree morphospecies recorded for forest plot censuses on BCNM.**

Matching to original garwood names (direct match) or to synonyms or alternate names that match to garwood.


#### 1. Direct match to garwood names

```{r bciplotdirectmatch}
# 1. direct name/binomial match
bci_plots_garwood_name_match <- census_list %>%
  filter(
    forestgeo_current_name %in% bcilist$orig_name |

    (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
       wcvp_matched_name %in% bcilist$wcvp_matched_name) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
       wcvp_accepted_name %in% bcilist$wcvp_accepted_name) |

    # match binomials
    (!is.na(orig_binomial) & (
        orig_binomial %in% bcilist$orig_binomial 
    )) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_binomial) & (
        wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% bcilist$wcvp_accepted_binomial 
    ))
  )

datatable(
  bci_plots_garwood_name_match %>% 
  select(sp6, forestgeo_current_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name), 
          caption="BCNM plot species matching directly to Garwood names",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


```
          
**Matched `r nrow(bci_plots_garwood_name_match)` species in the plots directly to garwood.**

#### 2. Alternate name match 
```{r plotalternatematchtry}


# 2. everything that did not match directly to garwood, match to alternate names
bci_plots_garwood_synonym_match <- census_list %>% 
  filter(
      !orig_name %in% bci_plots_garwood_name_match$orig_name &
    (
      orig_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key |
      
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
         wcvp_matched_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key) |
        
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
         wcvp_accepted_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key)
    )
  )


# 3. print the altname it matched to and why that name is in garwood

bci_plots_garwood_synonym_print <- bci_plots_garwood_synonym_match %>%
  # Join using a chain of coalesce to check the most reliable fields first
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("orig_name" = "match_key")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("orig_binomial" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_bi")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_name" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_match")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("wcvp_accepted_name" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_acc")) %>%
  
  # Now, condense everything into final fields
  mutate(
    orig_garwood    = coalesce(garwood_orig_match, garwood_orig_match_bi, garwood_orig_match_match, garwood_orig_match_acc),
    orig_garwood_synonym = coalesce(garwood_synonym_match, garwood_synonym_match_bi, garwood_synonym_match_match, garwood_synonym_match_acc),
    firstmatchtype  = coalesce(firstmatchtype, firstmatchtype_bi, firstmatchtype_match, firstmatchtype_acc),
    alternate_name  = coalesce(alt_name, alt_name_bi, alt_name_match, alt_name_acc),
    prefrered_name   = coalesce(pref_name, pref_name_bi, pref_name_match, pref_name_acc)
  ) %>%
  select(-ends_with("_bi"), -ends_with("_match"), -ends_with("_acc")) # Clean up extra columns

```

*Matched `r nrow(bci_plots_garwood_synonym_match)` species in the plots by synonyms or alternate names to garwood.*

The table below shows BCI plot species that could not be matched directly to Garwood (2009) but were linked through an alternate name. The `firstmatchtype` column indicates which name pairing produced the link:

Definitions:

- F=ForestGeo

- G=Garwood

- Fcur = ForestGeo current name from most recent list

- Falt = ForestGeo alternate name from most recent list for Panama woody plants in general

- Gorig = Garwood 2009 original name

- Galt = Garwood 2009 alternate name

- **direct synonym** — the name appears directly in the Garwood list as a synonym
 
- **Fcur-Gorig** — the current ForestGeo name matches a name listed by Garwood

- **Fcur-Gorigbi** — the current ForestGeo name matches the binomial of a Garwood original name.

- **Falt-Gorig** — a ForestGeo alternate name matches a name originally listed by Garwood

- **Falt-Gorigbi** — a ForestGeo alternate name matches the binomial of a Garwood original name.

- **Fcur-Galtbi** — the current ForestGeo name matches the binomial of a Garwood synonym

- **Falt-Galt** — a historical ForestGeo alternate name matches a synonym documented by Garwood

- **Falt-Galtbi** — a historical ForestGeo alternate name matches the binomial of a Garwood synonym

 

Note that "Garwood original name" refers to the name as it appears in the Garwood (2009) revision, not necessarily the currently accepted name.

```{r printaltmatches}
datatable(
  bci_plots_garwood_synonym_print %>% 
  select(sp6, forestgeo_current_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, firstmatchtype, orig_garwood,
         alternate_name, prefrered_name, orig_garwood_synonym), 
          caption="BCNM plot species where an alternate name of the species matched to garwood",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


```

Printing species in the plots that are not in Garwood. 
```{r allnomatchbciplot}

# combine direct matches and matches by alternate names or synonyms
bci_plots_garwood_match <- bind_rows(
  bci_plots_garwood_name_match,
  bci_plots_garwood_synonym_match
) %>%
  distinct(sp6, .keep_all = TRUE)

# filter unmatched rows
bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all <- census_list %>%
  filter(!sp6 %in% bci_plots_garwood_match$sp6)

kable(bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all  %>% select(sp6, census_name, forestgeo_current_name , wcvp_matched_name, 
                                              wcvp_accepted_name, census_plot), 
          caption="Species recorded in BCNM plots census that don't match anything in Garwood")



write_xlsx(bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all, FILECHECKPLOTS)

```

Added `r nrow(bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all)` completely new rows to the BCI list


```{r addplottobci}

bcilist$forestgeo_current_name = NA_character_


bcilist <- bind_rows(
  bcilist,
  bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all %>% 
    mutate(source_list="Plot_census",
           orig_name = forestgeo_current_name # the orig_name in bcilist represents the name originally listed in the source list.
           ) %>%
    select(all_of(c(listscol, "census_name", "in_census", "census_plot", "bci50ha", "bci10ha", "P11", "P14", "Zetek", "Pearson", "Drayton", "bci25ha", "sp6", "sp4", "forestgeo_current_name"))
))


```


### Lianas BCI list


Check whether the lianas are already in the list we have assembled so far: 

```{r lianasnobcilist}

lianas_list <- lianas_list %>% filter(isbci=="TRUE")
lianas_list <- lianas_list %>% distinct(orig_name, .keep_all = TRUE)
lianas_list <- lianas_list %>% 
  mutate(
    wcvp_accepted_binomial = paste(wcvp_accepted_name_genus, wcvp_accepted_name_species),
    former_binomial = ifelse(is.na(GenusFormer),NA,paste(GenusFormer,SpeciesOnlyFormer))) %>%
  
  mutate(
    former_name = paste(ifelse(is.na(SubspeciesFormer),former_binomial,
                                    paste(former_binomial,SubspeciesFormer))))

lianas_list$liana_name = lianas_list$orig_name
lianas_list$source_list = "Lianas"

datatable(
  lianas_list %>% group_by(wcvp_accepted_name) %>% filter(n()>1 &!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name)) %>% select(liana_name, former_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name),
  caption="Lianas with duplicated wcvp accepted name. Meaning that under WCVP they are the same species"
)

```

Senegalia hayesii is in the Current Forestgeo Panama woody plant list, while Senegalia Acanthopylla is not. This needs revision. Since Senegalia Acanthopylla is not being added to the list because its already in Garwood indirectly when matching with Senegalia hayesii.

Working with `r nrow(lianas_list)` liana species. 

#### 1. Direct match to the species already in the BCNM list.

```{r lianasdirect}

# 1. direct name match
lianas_list_directmatch <- lianas_list %>% 
  filter(orig_name %in% bcilist$orig_name) %>% 
  mutate(bcilist_name = orig_name)

# Match by wcvp matched name (the species name is slightly different)
lianas_list_wcvpmatch <- lianas_list %>% 
  filter(!orig_name %in% lianas_list_directmatch$orig_name) %>%
  left_join(bcilist %>% select(wcvp_matched_name, bcilist_name = orig_name), by="wcvp_matched_name") %>% filter(!is.na(bcilist_name))


lianas_direct_name_match <- bind_rows(
  lianas_list_directmatch,
  lianas_list_wcvpmatch
  )

datatable(
  lianas_direct_name_match %>%
    filter(!is.na(bcilist_name))%>%
    select(liana_name, bcilist_name, wcvp_matched_name), 
          caption="Liana species matching directly to the names in the list assembled so far.",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))  

bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  left_join(lianas_direct_name_match %>% select(liana_name, bcilist_name), by=c( "orig_name"="bcilist_name"))

```
          
*Matched `r nrow(lianas_direct_name_match)` species in the plots directly to the current BCNM list*


```{r lianasaltmatch}

# Create a look up table of the current list

bcilist_lookup <- bind_rows(
  bcilist %>% select(match_key =orig_name , bcilist_name=orig_name , bcilist_wcvp_accepted=wcvp_accepted_name,
                     source_list, garwood_name, zotz_name, forestgeo_current_name), 
  
  bcilist %>% select(match_key = orig_binomial, bcilist_name=orig_name , bcilist_wcvp_accepted=wcvp_accepted_name,
                     source_list, garwood_name, zotz_name, forestgeo_current_name), 
  
  bcilist %>% mutate(wcvp_matched_binomial = word(wcvp_matched_name,1,2)) %>%
    select(match_key = wcvp_matched_binomial, bcilist_name=orig_name , bcilist_wcvp_accepted=wcvp_accepted_name,
                     source_list, garwood_name, zotz_name, forestgeo_current_name), 
  
  bcilist %>% select(match_key = wcvp_matched_name,bcilist_name=orig_name , bcilist_wcvp_accepted=wcvp_accepted_name,
                     source_list, garwood_name, zotz_name, forestgeo_current_name), 
  
  bcilist %>% select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_name, bcilist_name=orig_name , bcilist_wcvp_accepted=wcvp_accepted_name, 
                     source_list, garwood_name, zotz_name, forestgeo_current_name), 
  
  bcilist %>% select(match_key = zotz_name, bcilist_name=orig_name , bcilist_wcvp_accepted=wcvp_accepted_name, source_list, garwood_name, zotz_name, forestgeo_current_name), 
  
  bcilist %>% select(match_key = forestgeo_current_name, bcilist_name=orig_name , bcilist_wcvp_accepted=wcvp_accepted_name, source_list, garwood_name, zotz_name, forestgeo_current_name), 
  
  bcilist %>% select(match_key = garwood_name, bcilist_name=orig_name , bcilist_wcvp_accepted=wcvp_accepted_name, source_list, garwood_name, zotz_name, forestgeo_current_name)
  
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  distinct(match_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
```

#### 2. Indirect name match 

Here we check if the lianas current or former name are already in the list indirectly, for example if the name matches a wcvp accepted name on the list.
```{r lianasalter}

lianas_list <- lianas_list %>% mutate(wcvp_matched_binomial = word(wcvp_matched_name,1,2)) 
# 2. everything that did not match directly to garwood, match to alternate names
lianas_garwood_indirect_match <- lianas_list %>% 
  filter(
      !liana_name %in% lianas_direct_name_match$liana_name &
    (
      orig_name %in% bcilist_lookup$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% bcilist_lookup$match_key |
        
      (!is.na(former_name) &
         former_name %in% bcilist_lookup$match_key) |
        
      (!is.na(former_binomial) &
         former_binomial %in% bcilist_lookup$match_key) |
      
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_binomial) &
         wcvp_matched_binomial %in% bcilist_lookup$match_key) |
        
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
         wcvp_matched_name %in% bcilist_lookup$match_key) |
        
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
         wcvp_accepted_name %in% bcilist_lookup$match_key)
    )
  )


# 3. print the altname it matched to and why that name is in garwood

lianas_indirect_match_print <- lianas_garwood_indirect_match %>%
  # Join using a chain of coalesce to check the most reliable fields first
  left_join(bcilist_lookup, by = c("orig_name" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_orig")) %>%
  
  left_join(bcilist_lookup, by = c("orig_binomial" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_bi")) %>%
  

  left_join(bcilist_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_name" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_match")) %>%
  
  left_join(bcilist_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_binomial" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_matchbi")) %>%
  
  left_join(bcilist_lookup, by = c("former_name" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_former")) %>%
  
  left_join(bcilist_lookup, by = c("former_binomial" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_formerbi")) %>%
  
  left_join(bcilist_lookup, by = c("wcvp_accepted_name" = "match_key"), 
            suffix = c("", "_acc")) %>%
  
  # Now, condense everything into final fields
  mutate(
    
    bcilist_name    = coalesce(
      bcilist_name,
      bcilist_name_bi,
      bcilist_name_match,
      bcilist_name_matchbi,
      bcilist_name_former,
      bcilist_name_formerbi,
      bcilist_name_acc),
      
          source_list    = coalesce(
      source_list_orig,
      source_list_bi,
      source_list_match,
      source_list_matchbi,
      source_list_former,
      source_list_formerbi,
      source_list_acc),
      
    bcilist_wcvp_accepted    = coalesce(
      bcilist_wcvp_accepted,
      bcilist_wcvp_accepted_bi,
      bcilist_wcvp_accepted_match,
      bcilist_wcvp_accepted_matchbi,
      bcilist_wcvp_accepted_former,
      bcilist_wcvp_accepted_formerbi,
      bcilist_wcvp_accepted_acc),
      
    garwood_name    = coalesce(
      garwood_name,
      garwood_name_bi,
      garwood_name_match,
      garwood_name_matchbi,
      garwood_name_former,
      garwood_name_formerbi,
      garwood_name_acc),
    
      zotz_name    = coalesce(
      zotz_name,
      zotz_name_bi,
      zotz_name_match,
      zotz_name_matchbi,
      zotz_name_former,
      zotz_name_formerbi,
      zotz_name_acc),
    
      forestgeo_current_name    = coalesce(
      forestgeo_current_name,
      forestgeo_current_name_bi,
      forestgeo_current_name_match,
      forestgeo_current_name_matchbi,
      forestgeo_current_name_former,
      forestgeo_current_name_formerbi,
      forestgeo_current_name_acc),
    
  ) %>%
  select(-ends_with("_orig"),-ends_with("_bi"), -ends_with("_match"), -ends_with("_matchbi"), 
         -ends_with("_former"),-ends_with("_formerbi"),-ends_with("_acc")) # Clean up extra columns

```

*Matched `r nrow(lianas_garwood_indirect_match)` species in lianas by synonyms or alternate names to garwood.*


```{r lianasprintaltmatches}
datatable(
  lianas_indirect_match_print %>% 
  select(liana_name, former_name, wcvp_accepted_name, bcilist_name, source_list,  garwood_name), 
          caption="lianas species that match indirectly to the current list (coming from Garwood)",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))
```


#### 3. Alternate name match

Next we see if the current name in the lianas list or the former name are alternate names of the species that are already in the list. 
```{r lianaaltmatch}

#4 match of former liana name to Garwood synonym
lianas_formertosynonym_match <- lianas_list %>%
  filter(
    !orig_name %in% c(lianas_direct_name_match$orig_name, lianas_garwood_indirect_match$orig_name) &
    (former_name %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_sp |
       former_binomial %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_binomial))

# see species that matched by a synonym 
lianas_formertosynonym_print <- lianas_formertosynonym_match %>%  left_join(
    garwood_synonyms %>% select(garwood_name = sp_name, synonym_sp, syn_text = SynonymTextALL) %>% distinct(),
    by = c("former_name" = "synonym_sp")
  ) 

kable(lianas_formertosynonym_print %>% 
            select(liana_name = orig_name, liana_former_name = former_name, garwood_name, 
                   liana_wcvp_accepted_name =wcvp_accepted_name,
                   garwood_synonym = syn_text), 
          caption="Liana species whose former names matched a Garwood synonym.",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

# 5. everything that did not match directly to garwood, match to alternate names
lianas_garwood_alternate_match <- lianas_list %>% 
  filter(
       !orig_name %in% c(lianas_direct_name_match$liana_name, lianas_garwood_indirect_match$orig_name) &
    (
      orig_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key |
      
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
         wcvp_matched_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key) |
        
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
         wcvp_accepted_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key)
    )
  )


# print the altname it matched to and why that name is in garwood

lianas_alternate_print <- lianas_garwood_alternate_match %>%
  # Join using a chain of coalesce to check the most reliable fields first
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("orig_name" = "match_key")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("orig_binomial" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_bi")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_name" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_match")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("wcvp_accepted_name" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_acc")) %>%
  
  # Now, condense everything into your final fields
  mutate(
    garwood_name    = coalesce(garwood_orig_match, garwood_orig_match_bi, garwood_orig_match_match, garwood_orig_match_acc),
    orig_garwood_synonym = coalesce(garwood_synonym_match, garwood_synonym_match_bi, garwood_synonym_match_match, garwood_synonym_match_acc),
    firstmatchtype  = coalesce(firstmatchtype, firstmatchtype_bi, firstmatchtype_match, firstmatchtype_acc),
    alternate_name  = coalesce(alt_name, alt_name_bi, alt_name_match, alt_name_acc),
    prefrered_name   = coalesce(pref_name, pref_name_bi, pref_name_match, pref_name_acc)
  ) %>%
  select(-ends_with("_bi"), -ends_with("_match"), -ends_with("_acc")) # Clean up extra columns

```

The table below shows liana species that could not be matched directly to Garwood (2009) but were linked through an alternate name. 

Note that "Garwood name" refers to the name as it appears in the Garwood (2009) revision, not necessarily the currently accepted name.

```{r showlianaalternate}
datatable(
  lianas_alternate_print %>% 
  select(liana_name = orig_name, garwood_name, garwood_synonym = orig_garwood_synonym), 
          caption="Liana species that matched to garwood by an alternate name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))
```


Add the lianas name to the BCI list for the species that matched
```{r addlianas}

lianas_garwood_match <- bind_rows(
  lianas_indirect_match_print,
  lianas_formertosynonym_print,
  lianas_alternate_print
) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, .keep_all = TRUE) 


# Updateand liana_name for species already in bcilist that matched by alternate name
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  left_join(lianas_garwood_match %>% select(liana_name_alt =liana_name, garwood_name), by="garwood_name")%>%
  mutate(
    liana_name = coalesce( liana_name, liana_name_alt)
  ) %>%
  select(-liana_name_alt)


```


Printing liana species that are not in the list so far and need to be added: 
```{r lianasnomatch}

lianas_no_garwood_at_all <- lianas_list %>%
  filter(!orig_name %in% bcilist$liana_name &
          !orig_name %in% lianas_garwood_match$orig_name 
           )


# 6. Output and Display
datatable(lianas_no_garwood_at_all %>%
  select(liana_former_name =former_name, 
         liana_name = orig_name, 
         liana_wcvp_matched_name = wcvp_matched_name,
         liana_wcvp_accepted_name = wcvp_accepted_name), 
          caption = "Lianas species recorded in the 50-ha plot that don't have a match in Garwood",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

write_xlsx(lianas_no_garwood_at_all, FILECHECKLIANAS)

```

Add to BCI list
```{r lianasbciadd}
lianas_no_garwood_at_all$source_list = "Lianas"
lianas_no_garwood_at_all$liana_name = lianas_no_garwood_at_all$orig_name

bcilist <- bind_rows(
  bcilist,
  lianas_no_garwood_at_all %>% select(all_of(listscol), "liana_name")
)


```

## Check consistency 
```{r savebotanists}

# Check consistency
# removing rows that are not identified at species level
kable(bcilist %>% filter(is.na(word(orig_binomial, 2))) %>% select(orig_name, wcvp_accepted_name), caption="Species to be removed from final list because they are not fully identified" )

bcilist <- bcilist %>% filter(!is.na(word(orig_binomial, 2))) 

bcidupnames <- bcilist %>% 
  filter(!is.na(orig_name)) %>%
  group_by(orig_name) %>% filter(n()> 1) 

if (nrow(bcidupnames)>0) {
  datatable(bcidupnames%>%
          select(orig_name, wcvp_accepted_name, source_list) %>% arrange(orig_name),
          caption="Species with duplicated original name",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
          )
  
}

bcidupwcvp <- bcilist %>% group_by(wcvp_accepted_name) %>% 
            filter(n()> 1)

datatable(
  bcidupwcvp %>% 
    filter(!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name)) %>%
    select(orig_name, wcvp_accepted_name, source_list)%>%
    arrange(wcvp_accepted_name), 
  caption="Species with the same wcvp_accepted_named as another species in the list",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
          )
  

datatable(
  bcidupwcvp %>% 
    filter(is.na(wcvp_accepted_name)) %>%
    select(orig_name, wcvp_accepted_name)%>%
    arrange(wcvp_accepted_name), 
  caption="No accepted name found",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
          )
  

bcilist %>%
  count(source_list) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  kable(col.names = c("source_list", "count"),caption="Sources of morphospecies in the BCI plant species list.")

datatable(
  bcilist %>%
    select(wcvp_accepted_name, orig_name, source_list) %>% 
    arrange(source_list),
    caption="Species list for BCI, with the source of each entry",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

```


# Use the Panama woody plant morphospecies list to link in current ForestGeo names and 6-letter and 4-letter species codes for all species.

The forestgeo name is the most up-to-date name for these species, as they are the names used by the botanists in Panama. The ForestgeoNames come from the current panama woody list. If the name in Garwood (or any of the list used here as a source for the names) is different from the name in ForestGeo, the ForestGeo name is preferred. We add new columns **"current name"** and  **"current_name_source"** with the preferred name and whether it is Garwood, Forestgeo, Zots or the Lianas list. 


We find the Forestgeo name that maps to the names in the list so far following the cases below. If it matches to one case, the current name is set to the ForestGeo current name and it will not be matched further to other cases. If the current ForestGeo name mapped to a species is already the current name of another species in the list, it will be ignored. Giving priority to the more direct matches.

For each case, we check if we have duplicated matches, that is species mapping to two different ForestGeo current names, and handle those depending on the match. 


- Case 0: if the species comes from the census of the plots, it already has its forestgeo name.
 
- Case 1: The species name is directly in forestgeo.

- Case 2: The species name is a former name to a current ForestGeo   name.

- Case 3: The species former name matches a current ForestGeo  name. Only matched when a unique ForestGeo name is found.

- Case 4: The species former name matches a former Forestgeo name.

- Case 5: The species binomial (genus + species, without subspecies) matches a current ForestGeo name.

- Case 6: The species binomial matches a former ForestGeo name.

- Case 7: The binomial of the species former name matches a former ForestGeo name.

- Case 8: The species WCVP accepted name or WCVP accepted binomial matches a current ForestGeo name or binomial. This handles cases where the name either in ForestGeo or our current BCI list is a synonym according to WCVP and has not been updated in one of the list, In this case we also leave the ForestGeo current name.

```{r renamingsome}
#checkpoint...
bcilist_orig <- bcilist


```


```{r addforestgeocurrentname}
bcilist <- bcilist_orig
# For all the species in the bcilist
# check if they are directly on the forestgeo /woody list
# check if they have an alternate name on that list

# Add a column with the forestgeo name
# Flag when the forestgeo_current_name != orig_garwood name

# 1. Create lookup table for woody species
forestgeo_names_lookup <- bind_rows(
  panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "Fcurr") %>% select(match_key = orig_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "wcvpmatch") %>% select(match_key = wcvp_matched_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
    panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "wcvpaccepted") %>% select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  # Group and collapse to keep provenance if a key matched in multiple ways
  group_by(match_key, forestgeo_current_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated) %>%
  summarise(
    matchby = paste(matchby, collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

forestgeo_names_lookup <- forestgeo_names_lookup %>% mutate(row_id = row_number())

### CHECKS ###
# check if one match_key points to different forestgeo_current_name
# This happens because the wcvp accepted name is different from the forestgeo_current_name. 
# The botanist are confident these species are different. But to wcvp v13 they are the same. 
duplicate_matchkey1 <- forestgeo_names_lookup %>%
  group_by(match_key) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(forestgeo_current_name) > 1 ) %>%
  arrange(match_key)

#datatable(duplicate_matchkey1,
#          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
#  caption="The same key maps to a different current name. For this cases the current #forestgeo name is different from the wcvp accepted name. And the current forestgeo name is #believed to be the accepted name.")

# In this case we remove the species were the match_key is different from the forestgeo_current_name.
alt_names_remove <- duplicate_matchkey1 %>% filter(forestgeo_current_name != match_key)

forestgeo_names_lookup <- forestgeo_names_lookup %>% filter(!row_id %in% alt_names_remove$row_id)

panwoody_synonyms <- panwoody_synonyms %>% select(sp6,
  forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, altname=SynonymTextSingle
)


```

```{r foresgeoadddirect}

# Prepare for the matches
bcilist <- bcilist %>% mutate(row_id = row_number(),
                           current_name = NA_character_, 
                           source_current_name = NA_character_
                              )

```  

### Case 0. The species comes from a forestgeo list

```{r case0matchfg}
# 0. The source of the names is the forestgeo list
case0_fgsource <- bcilist %>% filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>% 
                  mutate(current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
                         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
                         )

# The species that are uniquely in the plots and not in the garwood list already have their forestgeo name. 
datatable(case0_fgsource %>%
            select(sp6,sp4, orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list, current_name, source_current_name),
          caption="Species that are only in the plot list already have a foresgeo code",
           options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )


bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% case0_fgsource$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)


matched_species <- case0_fgsource
```

### Case 1. The species name is a current forestgeoname

```{r case1fgname}

case1_directmatch <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(forestgeo_names_lookup, by = c("orig_name"= "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_orig")) %>%
  
  left_join(forestgeo_names_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_name"= "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_wcvpmatch")) 



case1_directmatch <- case1_directmatch %>%  mutate(
    forestgeo_current_name =     coalesce(
      forestgeo_current_name,
      forestgeo_current_name_wcvpmatch,
    ),
    sp6 = coalesce(
      sp6,
      sp6_wcvpmatch,
    ),
    sp4 = coalesce(
      sp4,
      sp4_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig", "_wcvpmatch"))) 

  
case1_directmatch <- case1_directmatch %>%
  mutate(
         current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         )  %>%
  filter(!is.na(sp6) & 
           !orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name) 

case1_directmatch %>% group_by(forestgeo_current_name) %>% filter(n() > 1)

datatable(case1_directmatch %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list, matchby),
          caption="Species original name is a forestgeo_current_name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )



matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name & !orig_name %in% matched_species$current_name ) %>% select(-sp6)

```


### Case 2. The species original name matches to forestgeo alternate name

```{r case2_1}


case2_forestgeoalternate <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(alternate_names %>% filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>% select(altname, forestgeo_current_name= prefname , sp6), 
            by=c("orig_name" = "altname"),suffix = c("", "_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>% filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>% select(altname, forestgeo_current_name = prefname , sp6), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_name" = "altname"),suffix = c("", "_wcvpmatch")) 



```



```{r case2fgname}
datatable(case2_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(forestgeo_current_name)
                   ) %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name,forestgeo_current_name_wcvpmatch, source_list),
          caption="Species original name is an alternate name and maps to two forestgeo names",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )

case2_forestgeoalternate <- case2_forestgeoalternate %>%  mutate(
    forestgeo_current_name =     coalesce(
      forestgeo_current_name,
      forestgeo_current_name_wcvpmatch
    ),
    sp6 = coalesce(
      sp6,
      sp6_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig",  "_prefmatch", "_wcvpmatch"))) 



case2_forestgeoalternate <- case2_forestgeoalternate %>%
  mutate(
         current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         )%>%
  filter(!is.na(sp6) & 
           !orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )


datatable(case2_forestgeoalternate %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Species original name is a forestgeo alternate name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )


matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name & !orig_name %in% matched_species$current_name ) %>% select(-sp6)

```



### Case 3. The species alternate name matches to one forestgeo current name


```{r case3fgname}

# Here we have to be careful, a species can have multiple alt names...
case3_forestgeoalternate <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(alternate_names %>% 
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  %>%
              select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("orig_name" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>% 
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
            select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_name" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_wcvpmatch"))


case3_forestgeoalternate <- case3_forestgeoalternate %>% 
  mutate(
    altname = coalesce(
      altname,
      altname_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig", "_wcvpmatch")))

# Keep only the ones that have alternate names
case3_forestgeoalternate <- case3_forestgeoalternate %>% filter(!is.na(altname)) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, altname, .keep_all = TRUE) # clearing duplicates


# Find the forestgeo name that matches to that alternate name
case3_forestgeoalternate <- case3_forestgeoalternate %>%
  left_join(panwoody_list %>% select(forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6), by = c("altname" = "forestgeo_current_name"))


datatable(case3_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname,source_list),
          caption="Two or more alternate names for the species match a current forestgeo name.")

```


In this case,  Zanthoxylum kellermanii is in Garwood and not in Forestgeo. However Garwood notes it has two alternate names: Zanthoxylum panamense and Zanthoxylum riedelianum, both are in Forestgeo. Zanthoxylum panamense is already in the list and Zanthoxylum ridelianum gets matched by the WCVP accepted binomial later in this script.


```{r case3_2}

datatable(case3_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, source_list),
          caption="Alternate names for the species match a current forestgeo name.")

case3_forestgeoalternate <- case3_forestgeoalternate %>%
        filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
        group_by(orig_name) %>% 
        filter(n() == 1) %>%
        ungroup() %>%
  mutate(current_name = altname,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo",
         forestgeo_current_name = altname
         ) %>%   
  filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )

matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name & !orig_name %in% matched_species$current_name ) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)


```



### Case 4. The species alternate name matches to  one forestgeo alternate name
```{r case4fgname}

# Here we have to be careful, a species can have multiple alt names...
case4_forestgeoalternate <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(alternate_names %>% 
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
             select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("orig_name" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>%
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
            select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_name" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_wcvpmatch"))


case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>% 
  mutate(
    altname = coalesce(
      altname,
      altname_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig", "_wcvpmatch")))


# Keep only the ones that have alternate names
case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>% filter(!is.na(altname)) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, altname, .keep_all = TRUE) # clearing duplicates

```

Check the different cases:

```{r checkcase4}
# Find the forestgeo alternate name that matches to that alternate name
case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>%
              filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>%
              select(forestgeo_current_name = prefname,
                     altname,  sp6), by = "altname")


datatable(case4_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Two or more alternate names for the species match an alternate forestgeo name.")

# keeping distinct orig_name, forestgeo_current_name pairs
# If the forestgeo name is the same as the orig_name also keep that option.
case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>%
  distinct(orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%# clearing duplicates
  filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
  group_by(orig_name) %>% 
  filter(n() == 1 | word(orig_name,2) == word(forestgeo_current_name,2)) %>%
  ungroup()
```

In this case we keep the forestgeo current name if it is the same for both alternate names. For Psychotria hebeclada we keep the one were the species is the same as the orig_name (because it was only a change in genus)

```{r checkcase4_1}
datatable(case4_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="One alternate name for the species match an alternate forestgeo name.")


case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>% 
          filter(!is.na(sp6))  %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
    mutate(current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
  filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )
 
matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name & !orig_name %in% matched_species$current_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)


```

So far we have the forestgeo names of `r nrow(matched_species)` out of `r nrow(bcilist)`

### Case 5. The binomial of the species matches a binomial in forestgeo current names
```{r binomialcase}
# Create binomial lookup
forestgeo_binomial_lookup <- bind_rows(
  forestgeo_names_lookup,
  panwoody_list %>%
    mutate(matchby = "origbinomial") %>%
    select(match_key = orig_binomial, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  panwoody_list %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpacceptedbinomial") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_binomial, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby)
) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  # Group and collapse to keep provenance if a key matched in multiple ways
  group_by(match_key, forestgeo_current_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated) %>%
  summarise(
    matchby = paste(matchby, collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

case5_binomials <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>%
  left_join(
  forestgeo_binomial_lookup, 
  by=c("orig_binomial" = "match_key"), 
  relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(!is.na(sp6))

case5_binomials <- case5_binomials %>%
  # Calculate mapping counts for both sides
  group_by(orig_name) %>%
  mutate(n_orig = n()) %>%
  group_by(forestgeo_current_name) %>%
  mutate(n_fg = n()) %>%
  ungroup() 

datatable(case5_binomials %>%
            filter(n_orig > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Species matched to two or more different forestgeo binomials. This means forestgeo makes a distinction between two subspecies")

# in this case, since both subspecies are different. Keep the one without the subspecies
case5_binomials <- case5_binomials %>% 
  filter(n_orig == 1 | (n_orig > 1 & is.na(word(forestgeo_current_name,3))))
```

In this case we keep the forestgeo name without the subspecies


```{r checkcase5_2}
datatable(case5_binomials %>%
            filter(n_fg > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="The same forestgeo binomial matched to different species name. This means forestgeo does not make a distinction between two subspecies")

case5_binomials <- case5_binomials %>% 
  filter(n_fg == 1 | 
        (n_fg > 1 & word(orig_name,2) == word(orig_name,4)) |
         orig_name == "Clitoria javitensis var. portobellensis"
        )
```

In this case, we keep the forestgeo name if in the original name, the subspecies is the same as the species. And in the case of Clitoria javitensis we keep the forestgeo name for Clitoria javitensis var. portobellensis

```{r checkcase5}
datatable(case5_binomials %>%
            group_by(orig_name,forestgeo_current_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="The species binomial found a match in forestgeo")

# Keep the distinct pairs of orig_name, forestgeo name
# If they are not distinct, keep only the ones were subspecies is the same as the species. 

case5_binomials <- case5_binomials %>%
          filter(
            !is.na(sp6))  %>%
            group_by(orig_name, forestgeo_current_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
    mutate(
      current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
      source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
      filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )

matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials)


```

Now we have the forestgeo names of `r nrow(matched_species)` out of `r nrow(bcilist)`



### Case 6. The species original binomial matches to a forestgeo alternate name

```{r case6_1}

bcilist<- bcilist %>%
  mutate(wcvp_matched_binomial = paste0(word(wcvp_matched_name, 1), " ",word(wcvp_matched_name, 2) ))
    
bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name & !orig_name %in% matched_species$current_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)
                                  
                                        
case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(alternate_names %>% filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>% select(altname, forestgeo_current_name= prefname , sp6), 
            by=c("orig_binomial" = "altname"),suffix = c("", "_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>% filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>% select(altname, forestgeo_current_name = prefname , sp6), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_binomial" = "altname"),suffix = c("", "_wcvpmatch")) 
```



```{r case6_2}

case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate <- case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate %>%  mutate(
    forestgeo_current_name =     coalesce(
      forestgeo_current_name,
      forestgeo_current_name_wcvpmatch
    ),
    sp6 = coalesce(
      sp6,
      sp6_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig",  "_prefmatch", "_wcvpmatch"))) 



case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate <- case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate %>%
  mutate(
         current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         )%>%
  filter(!is.na(sp6) & 
           !orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )


datatable(case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Species binomial name is a forestgeo alternate name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )

case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate <- case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate %>% select(-wcvp_matched_binomial)

matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials, case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name & !orig_name %in% matched_species$current_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)

```


### Case 7. The species binomial alternate names matches to a forestgeo alternate name

```{r case6fgname}

# Here we have to be careful, a species can have multiple alt names...
case7_binomialalt <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>%
  mutate(wcvp_matched_binomial = paste0(word(wcvp_matched_name, 1), " ", word(wcvp_matched_name, 2) )) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>% 
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
             select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("orig_binomial" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>%
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
            select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_binomial" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_wcvpmatch"))


case7_binomialalt <- case7_binomialalt %>% 
  mutate(
    altname = coalesce(
      altname,
      altname_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig", "_wcvpmatch")))


# Keep only the ones that have alternate names
case7_binomialalt <- case7_binomialalt %>% filter(!is.na(altname)) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, altname, .keep_all = TRUE) # clearing duplicates

```

Check the different cases:

```{r case6binomialas}
# Find the forestgeo alternate name that matches to that alternate name
case7_binomialalt <- case7_binomialalt %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>%
              filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>%
              select(forestgeo_current_name = prefname,
                     altname,  sp6), by = "altname")


datatable(case7_binomialalt %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Two or more alternate names for the species match an alternate forestgeo name.")
```

```{r case6binomialas_1}
datatable(case7_binomialalt %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
```
