```{r case6binomialas_1}
          caption="One alternate name for the species match an alternate forestgeo name.")


case7_binomialalt <- case7_binomialalt %>% 
          filter(!is.na(sp6))  %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
    mutate(current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
  filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )
 
matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials, case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate, case7_binomialalt)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name & !orig_name %in% matched_species$current_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)


```

So far we have the forestgeo names of `r nrow(matched_species)` out of `r nrow(bcilist)`


### Case 8. The species accepted name according to WCVPv13 matches to Forestgeo.

So far we have matched with the original name or the wcvp matched name (to prevent mismatches due to spelling differences). 

Next we try to match the species that have not matched yet by the wcvp_accepted_name. 


```{r case6directwcvp}

case8_directwcvp <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(forestgeo_names_lookup, by = c("wcvp_accepted_name"= "match_key"))


case8_directwcvp <- case8_directwcvp %>%
  filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
  mutate(forestgeo_current_name = wcvp_accepted_name,
         current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
      filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )

case8_directwcvp %>% group_by(forestgeo_current_name) %>% filter(n() > 1)

datatable(case8_directwcvp %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list, matchby),
          caption="Species wcvp accepted name matches a forestgeo name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )




matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials, case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate, case7_binomialalt, case8_directwcvp)


bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name& !orig_name %in% matched_species$current_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)

```

Matching by WCVP accepted binomial
```{r case6wcvpbinomial}

case8_binomialwcvp <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>%
  left_join(
  forestgeo_binomial_lookup, 
  by=c("wcvp_accepted_binomial" = "match_key"), 
  relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(!is.na(sp6))

datatable(case8_binomialwcvp %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Species original wcvp accepted binomial matches a forestgeo binomial",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )

case8_binomialwcvp <- case8_binomialwcvp %>%
          filter(
            !is.na(sp6))  %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            group_by(forestgeo_current_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
    mutate(
      current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
      source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
      filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )
matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials, case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate, case7_binomialalt, case8_directwcvp, case8_binomialwcvp)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name & !orig_name %in% matched_species$current_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)
```




## Consolidate current_name:
Next we do two things to the current names: 

1. Add the WCVP info for the newly added ForestGeo names

2. For the cases that did not find a ForestGeo name, set the original name in the source list as the current name.

```{r joineverything}
 
cols_to_keep <- colnames(bcilist)

# Adding codes and the WCVP columns mission from the Forestgeo current source
cols_to_replace <- c("sp4","orig_habit", 
                     "orig_author", "wcvp_matched_authors",
                     "wcvp_accepted_binomial", "wcvp_matched_name", 
                     "wcvp_matched_plant_name_id", "wcvp_matched_ipni_id", 
                     "wcvp_matched_status", "wcvp_accepted_name", 
                     "wcvp_accepted_family", "wcvp_accepted_authority", 
                     "wcvp_accepted_plant_name_id", "wcvp_accepted_powo_id", 
                     "wcvp_accepted_ipni_id", "wcvp_accepted_lifeform")

matched_species <- matched_species %>%
  # Remove existing versions of these columns
  select(-any_of(cols_to_replace)) %>%
  # Join and pull only those columns from the source
  left_join(panwoody_list %>% select(sp6, all_of(cols_to_replace)), by = "sp6") %>%
  # change name of orig_habit
  rename(
    forestgeo_habit = orig_habit
    )


bcilist_fgnames <- bind_rows(
  matched_species %>% select(all_of(cols_to_keep), forestgeo_habit),
  bcilist %>%
    filter(!orig_name %in% c(matched_species$orig_name)
           )
)


# check for duplicated current names
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
          filter(!is.na(current_name)) %>%
          group_by(current_name) %>%
          filter(n() > 1) %>%  
          select(sp6,sp4, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name, source_list) %>% arrange(current_name),
          caption="Current name is duplicated",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )
```


So far we have found the best matches for forestgeo names. If a species is left unmatched to forestgeo it is because it is in no way in the current panama woody list, or because the match created a conflict with other species in the list that had a more direct match to the same forestgeoname (e.g it was a direct match of name while the other matched through a binomial or synonym)

Now for the species we couldnt find a match for, we set the current name as the name they have in their original list. And check again if this could potentially create a conflict with the forestgeo names. In which case the species needs revision.

```{r currentname}

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>% 
  mutate(current_name = ifelse(is.na(current_name),
                               orig_name,
                               current_name
                               ),
         source_current_name = ifelse(is.na(source_current_name),
                               source_list,
                               source_current_name
                               ),
         )

datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
            filter(!is.na(current_name)) %>%
          group_by(current_name) %>%
          filter(n() > 1) %>%  
          select(sp6,sp4, current_name, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name, source_current_name, source_list ) %>% arrange(current_name),
          caption="Current name is duplicated",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )

```


# Fix notation for Hybrids
The correct notation for hybrids should use the character instead of "x" or "X"
```{r fixhybrid}

# Fix the hybrid notation with the correct x 
bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>% 
  mutate(current_name = ifelse(str_detect(current_name, " x | x | × "),
                               paste0(word(current_name,1), " × ", word(current_name,3)),
                               current_name))

```

# Remove subspecies and varieties

Subspecies and varieties will be omited from the final list. Keeping only the binomial. Except for Swartzia simplex var. continentalis and Swartzia simplex var. grandiflora that are very different and are recognized as distinct varieties by the Forestgeo botanists. 

```{r removesp} 
bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>% mutate(
          current_binomial = ifelse(str_detect(current_name, " x | x | × "),
                                    current_name, # keep the full name 
                                    word(current_name, 1,2)))

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>% add_count(current_binomial, name="binomials")

datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
            filter(!is.na(word(current_name, 4))) %>% 
            select(current_name, current_binomial, source_current_name),
            caption = "Subspecies in the list" )

datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>% 
        filter(binomials > 1) %>% 
        select(sp6, current_name, source_current_name),
        caption="Subspecies in the list that have the same binomial as another species in the list")


subspecies_to_keep <- c("Swartzia simplex var. continentalis",
                        "Swartzia simplex var. grandiflora")

# keep only one species binomial, preferably the one with forestgeo name. 

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  # for swartzia simplex leave the current name as binomial
  mutate(
    
    current_binomial = ifelse(current_name %in% subspecies_to_keep |
                              word(current_name,2) == "×", 
                              current_name, 
                              word(current_name, 1,2)
                              ),
    
    status_priority = case_when(
      source_current_name == "ForestGeo"~ 1,
      TRUE~ 2
    )
  )  %>% add_count(current_binomial, name="binomials")


datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>% filter(binomials > 1) %>% select(sp6, current_name, source_current_name), caption="Species with duplicate binomials")

# Removing the duplicate binomials
bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames%>%
  group_by(current_binomial) %>%
  slice_min(status_priority, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>% select(sp6, current_binomial, source_current_name), 
          caption="Species list after removing duplicate binomials")
```


# Keeping only the binomials

Since the binomials will be used, we set the wcvp columns to the binomial values (the ids and everything needs to map to the binomial names)
```{r}

species_fix <- bcilist_fgnames %>% filter(
  current_name != current_binomial &
  current_binomial != wcvp_matched_name
) %>% select(-wcvp_matched_binomial, -wcvp_accepted_binomial)

species_fix <- species_fix %>% 
  left_join(
    garwood_list %>% 
      select(orig_name_garwood = orig_name, ends_with("_binomial")), 
    by = c("current_name"="orig_name_garwood")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    zotz_list %>% 
      select(orig_name_zotz = orig_name, ends_with("_binomial")), 
    by = c("current_name"="orig_name_zotz"),
    suffix = c("", ".zotz")
  )

wcvp_base_cols <- c(
        # columns of matched name
        "wcvp_matched_name", "wcvp_matched_rank", "wcvp_matched_status", "wcvp_matched_plant_name_id", "wcvp_matched_ipni_id", "match_similarity",
        
        # columns of accepted name
        "wcvp_accepted_plant_name_id","wcvp_accepted_powo_id", "wcvp_accepted_ipni_id",
        "wcvp_accepted_name", "wcvp_accepted_name_genus",  "wcvp_accepted_name_species",
        "wcvp_accepted_name_rank", "wcvp_accepted_family", 
        "wcvp_accepted_name_infraspecific_rank", "wcvp_accepted_name_infraspecies",
        "wcvp_accepted_author", "wcvp_accepted_authority",
        "wcvp_lifeform_description"
        )

# 2. Perform the update logic
species_fix <- species_fix %>%
  mutate(across(any_of(wcvp_base_cols), ~ {
    # Dynamically find the matching binomial column name
    # e.g., if column is 'wcvp_matched_name', look for 'wcvp_matched_name_binomial'
    bin_col <- paste0(cur_column(), "_binomial")
    
    # Conditional replacement based on the source
    case_when(
      source_current_name == "Garwood2009" ~ get(bin_col),
      source_current_name == "Zotz"        ~ get(paste0(bin_col, ".zotz")),
      TRUE                                 ~ .  # Keep existing value if not Garwood/Zotz
    )
  })) %>%
  # 3. Clean up: Remove all the temporary .garwood and .zotz columns
  select(-contains(".garwood"), -contains(".zotz"))



# Clean up the binomial columns we used for the swap
species_fix_final <- species_fix %>%
  select(all_of(colnames(bcilist_fgnames))) # Keep only columns that exist in the main list


# Update bcilist_fgnames with the fixed rows
bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  rows_update(species_fix_final, by = "current_name")


```




# Save BCI Botanist list

## Sanity checks
```{r sanitycheck}
# Sanity check species that have a forestgeo current name that is not in forestego
bcilist_fgnames %>% filter(source_current_name == "ForestGeo" & !current_name %in% panwoody_list$orig_name)
```


## Add plot presence
Now that we have the Forestgeo current names for all the species in the list, we match to the plot info:

```{r addplotinof}

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  # 1. Remove the existing columns so there's no conflict
  select(-any_of(c("census_name", "census_plot", "in_census", "Other_BCNM_plots", "BCI"))) %>%
  # 2. Join the fresh data from census_list
  left_join(
    census_list %>% select(sp6, census_name, census_plot, in_census, BCI, Other_BCNM_plots),
    by = "sp6"
  )

```

See species in the plots that have codes that ended up NOT being in the final list. These species 
```{r sanitycheck2}
# Sanity check species that have a forestgeo current name that is not in forestego

species_census_missing <- census_list %>% filter(!sp6 %in% bcilist_fgnames$sp6)


datatable(
bci_plots_garwood_synonym_print %>% 
  filter(sp6 %in%  species_census_missing$sp6) %>%
  select(sp6, census_plot, forestgeo_current_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, firstmatchtype, orig_garwood,
         alternate_name, prefrered_name, orig_garwood_synonym),
  
          caption="Species in census that are NOT In the final list")

write_xlsx(
  bci_plots_garwood_synonym_print %>% 
  filter(sp6 %in%  species_census_missing$sp6) %>%
  select(sp6, census_plot, forestgeo_current_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, firstmatchtype, orig_garwood,
         alternate_name, prefrered_name, orig_garwood_synonym),
  "tocheck/species_census_missing.xlsx"
)
```


## Add woody column
```{r addwoody field}

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  mutate(
    # If it has an sp6 code then it is woody or if the binomial is in woody too. 
    woody_forestgeo = !is.na(sp6) |
      orig_binomial %in% forestgeo_binomial_lookup$match_key | 
      wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% forestgeo_binomial_lookup$match_key |
      current_name %in% forestgeo_names_lookup$match_key |
      !is.na(forestgeo_current_name)
  )

colnames(bcilist_fgnames)

# Species in the woody list because they have sp6 code
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(woody_forestgeo & !is.na(sp6)) %>%
  select(sp6, sp4, current_name, 
         wcvp_accepted_name, 
         wcvp_accepted_lifeform), 
  caption="BCI species that are in the woody list because their name is in Forestgeo",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


# Species in the woody list because their binomial is in forestgeo
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(woody_forestgeo & is.na(sp6)) %>%
  select(current_name, source_current_name, 
         wcvp_accepted_name, 
         wcvp_accepted_lifeform), 
  caption="BCI species that are in the woody list because their binomial is in forestgeo",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>% mutate(garwood_lifeform = LF_RF)



```


## Add GBIF ID

To find the GBIF id we will use the WCVP matched name (which hopefully does not have a different spelling in GBIF so we can have a perfect match)

```{r addgibfid}

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number()) 
  
  
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(is.na(wcvp_matched_name)) %>%
  select(current_name), 
  caption="Current name does not have WCVP match")


# Build name input from WCVP matched name
names_for_gbif <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(!is.na(wcvp_matched_name)) %>%
  distinct(wcvp_matched_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(full_name_auth = paste(wcvp_matched_name, coalesce(wcvp_matched_authors, ""))) %>% 
  rename(scientificName = full_name_auth)

# Query GBIF backbone
gbif_backbone_matched <- name_backbone_checklist(names_for_gbif, kingdom = "Plantae")

# Check match quality
gbif_match_summary <- gbif_backbone_matched %>% 
  count(matchType)

print(gbif_match_summary)

# How many got a usable GBIF ID?
gbif_backbone_matched %>% 
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    exact_match = sum(matchType == "EXACT", na.rm = TRUE),
    fuzzy_match = sum(matchType == "FUZZY", na.rm = TRUE),
    no_match    = sum(matchType == "NONE", na.rm = TRUE)
  )


gbif_redo <- gbif_backbone_matched %>% 
            filter(
              is.na(matchType) |
              !matchType %in% c("EXACT")) 

# See the ones that got a match by Variant or Higher rank
datatable(gbif_redo %>%
            select(verbatim_name, scientificName, matchType), 
          caption="Species not matching exactly (verbatim refers to the fileds in the BCNM list)",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

```

In some cases the GBIF  match is not EXACT, in this case we redo the search using the author from the original dataset. 

```{r redogbifid}
# in these cases we match again using the orig_author instead of the wcvp author. 
names_for_gbif <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter( current_name %in% gbif_redo$verbatim_current_name) %>%
  mutate(full_name_auth = paste(wcvp_matched_name, coalesce(orig_author, ""))) %>% 
  mutate(scientificName = full_name_auth)

# Query GBIF backbone
gbif_backbone_matched2 <- name_backbone_checklist(names_for_gbif, kingdom = "Plantae")

# Check match quality
gbif_match_summary <- gbif_backbone_matched2 %>% 
  count(matchType)
print(gbif_match_summary)
datatable(gbif_backbone_matched2 %>%
            select(verbatim_name, scientificName, matchType), 
          caption="Species not matching exactly (verbatim refers to the fileds in the BCNM list)",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))
```

The Higher rank cases get resolved and only the VARIANT names remain, in this cases is because they have slightly different spelling, so we can leave the id of the variant names. 


```{r}

# remove the cases we redid
gbif_backbone_matched <- gbif_backbone_matched %>% 
  filter(!verbatim_current_name %in% gbif_backbone_matched2$verbatim_current_name)

# bind rows
gbif_backbone_matched <- bind_rows(
  gbif_backbone_matched, 
  gbif_backbone_matched2
)

```


```{r checknonacceptedgbif}
datatable(gbif_backbone_matched %>% 
            filter(!status %in% c("ACCEPTED")) %>%
            select(verbatim_current_name, scientificName, matchType, verbatim_wcvp_matched_status, status,  verbatim_wcvp_accepted_name, acceptedScientificName), 
          caption="Species current name in the list is not accepted according to GBIF (verbatim refers to the fileds in the BCNM list)",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

```
The matched name can be a synonym, WCVP v13 also indicated that, but it is the name we will be using for current_name in the list, as the accepted name has not been reviewed by the STRI botanists in Panama.


We add the GBIF id and the GBIF scientificName as columns to our dataset

```{r addgbifmatchednames}
bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  mutate(
    row_id = as.character(row_id)
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    gbif_backbone_matched %>% 
      select(
        row_id = verbatim_row_id, 
        wcvp_matched_name_gbif_id = usageKey, 
        wcvp_matched_name_gbif_scientificName = scientificName,
        wcvp_matched_name_gbif_matchtype = matchType,
        wcvp_matched_name_gbif_status = status,
        wcvp_matched_name_gbif_acceptedScientificName = acceptedScientificName
      ),
    by = "row_id"
  )
```


We also find the GBIF id for the **accepted name.** 

```{r addgibfidaccepted}
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(is.na(wcvp_accepted_name)) %>%
  select(current_name), 
  caption="Current name does not have WCVP accepted name")


# Build name input from WCVP matched name
names_for_gbif <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name)) %>%
  mutate(full_name_auth = paste(wcvp_accepted_name, coalesce(wcvp_accepted_authority, ""))) %>% 
  rename(scientificName = full_name_auth)

# Query GBIF backbone
gbif_backbone_accepted <- name_backbone_checklist(names_for_gbif, kingdom = "Plantae")

# Check match quality
gbif_match_summary <- gbif_backbone_accepted %>% 
  count(matchType)

print(gbif_match_summary)

# How many got a usable GBIF ID?
gbif_backbone_accepted %>% 
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    exact_match = sum(matchType == "EXACT", na.rm = TRUE),
    fuzzy_match = sum(matchType == "FUZZY", na.rm = TRUE),
    no_match    = sum(matchType == "NONE", na.rm = TRUE)
  )


# See the ones that got a match by Variant or Higher rank
datatable(gbif_backbone_accepted %>% 
            filter(
              is.na(matchType) |
              !matchType %in% c("EXACT")) %>% 
            select(verbatim_name, scientificName), 
          caption="Species not matching exactly (verbatim refers to the fileds in the BCNM list)",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

datatable(gbif_backbone_accepted %>% 
            filter(!status %in% c("ACCEPTED")) %>%
            select(verbatim_current_name, verbatim_wcvp_matched_status, status, verbatim_wcvp_accepted_name=verbatim_name, acceptedScientificName), 
          caption="Species WCVP accepted name in the list is not accepted according to GBIF (verbatim refers to the fileds in the BCNM list)",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))
```
Surprisingly, the wcvp_accepted name can also be a Synonym according to GBIF, this can happen because GBIF gets the accepted names from different sources. We will add the GBIF accepted name to the list as an additional column.

See the ids that are not EXCAT or missing
```{r}
datatable(
  gbif_backbone_accepted %>% filter(is.na(usageKey) | matchType != "EXACT") %>% 
    select(verbatim_current_name, verbatim_wcvp_accepted_name)
)
```


We add the GBIF id and the GBIF scientificName as columns to our dataset

```{r addgbifacceptednames}
bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  left_join(
    gbif_backbone_accepted %>% 
      select(
        row_id = verbatim_row_id, 
        wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_id = usageKey, 
        wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_scientificName = scientificName,
        wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_matchtype = matchType,
        wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_status = status,
        wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_acceptedScientificName = acceptedScientificName
      ),
    by = "row_id"
  )
```

See the names that dont have an exact match: 
```{r noexact}
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>% filter(is.na(wcvp_matched_name_gbif_matchtype) |
                             wcvp_matched_name_gbif_matchtype != "EXACT") %>% select(current_name, wcvp_matched_name_gbif_matchtype, wcvp_matched_name_gbif_scientificName, wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_matchtype, wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_scientificName),
          caption="Names that dont have an exact match to GBIF id")
```


```{r noexact2}
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>% filter(is.na(wcvp_matched_name_gbif_matchtype) |
                             wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_matchtype != "EXACT") %>% select(current_name, wcvp_matched_name_gbif_matchtype, wcvp_matched_name_gbif_scientificName, wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_matchtype, wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_scientificName),
          caption="Names that dont have an exact match to GBIF id")
```



## Create dictionary of the columns and add metadata

In the final excel file, there will be one sheet with the dictionary of the columns, and another with metadata of the source files of this list. 

```{r columns_description}

plot_pattern <- "bci50ha|bci10ha|P14|P11|Zetek|bci25ha|AVA|Drayton|Pearson"

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  arrange(current_name) %>%
  mutate(
    BCI = case_when(
      # If it is in Zotz or Lianas then it is in BCI
      source_list %in% c("Zotz", "Lianas") ~ TRUE,
      
      # If it is in Garwood and the column Other_BCM_plots is false its in BCI
      (!is.na(garwood_name) & !is.na(garwood_BCNM) & !garwood_BCNM) ~ TRUE,
      
      # If it is in any of the BCI plots
      str_detect(census_plot, plot_pattern) ~ TRUE,
      
      # Default to FALSE if none of the above match
      TRUE ~ FALSE
    ),

    
  ) %>%
  rename(
    wcvp_match_similarity = match_similarity,
    current_authority = orig_author
  )


column_descriptions <- list(
  current_binomial = "Current binomial of the species. This is the name that should be used (binomial except for Swartzia simplex which is a variety recognized by the STRI botanists)",
  current_name               = "Current name for the species",
  source_current_name        = "Source of the current name",
  current_authority = "Authority of the current name from the source list",
    
  
  # BCI plot presence
  sp6                        = "Current Sp6 code of the species in Forestgeo",
  sp4                        = "Current Sp4 code of the species in Forestgeo",
  
  # WCVP matched fields
  wcvp_matched_authors = "Authority of the matched name in WCVP",
  wcvp_matched_name          = "Species name as matched in the World Checklist of Vascular Plants (WCVP)",
  wcvp_matched_plant_name_id = "WCVP internal plant name ID for the matched name",
  wcvp_matched_ipni_id       = "IPNI identifier for the matched name in WCVP",
  wcvp_matched_status        = "Taxonomic status of the matched name in WCVP (e.g. accepted, synonym)",
  wcvp_match_similarity = "Score of similarity between name in the list and the name in WCVP according to WCVP",
  
  # WCVP accepted fields
  wcvp_accepted_name         = "Currently accepted species name according to WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_binomial     = "Accepted genus + species binomial according to WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_family       = "Accepted family according to WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_authority    = "Taxonomic authority for the accepted name in WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_plant_name_id = "WCVP internal plant name ID for the accepted name",
  wcvp_accepted_powo_id      = "Plants of the World Online (POWO) identifier for the accepted name",
  wcvp_accepted_ipni_id      = "IPNI identifier for the accepted name in WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_lifeform     = "Life form classification of the species according to WCVP",
  
  # Source and Garwood
  garwood_name               = "Species name as it appears in Garwood (2009)",
  garwood_synonyms           = "Synonyms listed for this species in Garwood (2009)",
  garwood_lifeform           = "Life form of the species as classified in Garwood (2009)",
  
  # Zotz
  zotz_name                  = "Species name as it appears in the Zotz epiphyte list",

  # Census plot list
  census_plot                = "Census plot(s) in which this species has been recorded",
  
  BCI = "Species was recorded in one of the BCI plots (bci50ha, bci10ha, P14, P11, Zetek, bci25ha, AVA, Drayton, Pearson) or they are in BCI according to the Lianas list, Garwood or Zotz",
  
  garwood_BCNM = "According to Gardwood the species is not on BCI but is in the BCNM",

  
  # Lianas
  liana_name                = "Species name as it appears in the lianas",
  

  woody_forestgeo            = "Whether the species is classified as woody according to presence in Forestgeo or binomial presence in Forestgeo",
  forestgeo_habit = "Habit coming from the forestgeo list",
  
  source_list = "Original source from where this species originates to create the list",
  
  
  # GBIF columns
  wcvp_matched_name_gbif_id = "GBIF id of the wcvp_matched_name derived from the usageKey", 
  wcvp_matched_name_gbif_scientificName = "Scientific name of wcvp_matched_name according to GBIF",
  wcvp_matched_name_gbif_matchtype = "Matched type of GBIF scientific name and wcvp_matched_name",
  wcvp_matched_name_gbif_status = "Status of wcvp_matched_name according to GBIF",
  wcvp_matched_name_gbif_acceptedScientificName = "Accepted scientific name of the wcvp_matched_name according to GBIF. Is NA if the gbif scientific name is accepted",
  
  wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_id = "GBIF id of the wcvp_accepted_name derived from the usageKey", 
  wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_scientificName = "Scientific name of wcvp_accepted_name according to GBIF",
  wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_matchtype = "Matched type of GBIF scientific name and wcvp_accepted_name",
  wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_status = "Status of wcvp_accepted_name according to GBIF",
  wcvp_accepted_name_gbif_acceptedScientificName = "Accepted scientific name of the wcvp_accepted_name according to GBIF. Is NA if the gbif scientific name is accepted"
)

dict_columns <- data.frame(
  column      = names(column_descriptions),
  description = unlist(column_descriptions),
  row.names   = NULL
)


# Metadata sheet
metadata_df <- data.frame(
  item        = c(
    "Report",
    "Date created",
    "Garwood list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Garwood synonyms",
    "Zotz epiphytes list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Lianas list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Panama Biota list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Panama woody species list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Panama woody synonyms",
    "Alternate names",
    "Alternate names (Garwood subset)"
  ),
  description = c(
    "A detailed report of the creation of this list can be found at: https://github.com/PanamaForestGEO/Panama_plant_species_lists/tree/master/reports",
    format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d"),
    FILE_GARWOOD,
    FILE_GARWOOD_SYN,
    FILE_ZOTZ,
    FILE_LIANAS,
    FILE_PBIOTA,
    FILE_PANWOODY,
    FILE_PANWOODY_SYN,
    FILE_ALTNAMES,
    FILE_ALTNAMES_GARWOOD
  )
)

write_xlsx(
  list(
    "BCNM List"   = bcilist_fgnames %>% select(all_of(dict_columns$column)),
    "Dictionary" = dict_columns,
    "Metadata"   = metadata_df
  ),
  FILECOMBINEDLIST
)

datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>% 
             select(all_of(dict_columns$column)), 
  caption="BCNM BOTANISTS SPECIES LIST",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

```


```{r posibblywoody}
wcvp_lifeform_woody_list = c(
  "tree",
  "shrub",
  "liana",
  "climbing shrub",
  "shrub or tree",
  "climbing shrub or tree",
  "scrambling shrub or tree",
  "subshrub or shrub",
  "subshrub, shrub or tree",
  "scrambling shrub",
  "perennial or shrub",
  "scrambling subshrub or shrub",
  "epiphytic shrub",
  "scrambling tree",
  "climbing shrub or liana",
  "scrambling shrub or liana",
  "tuberous shrub or tree",
  "herbaceous tree",
  "climbing herbaceous tree",
  "climbing herbaceous tree or liana",
  "climbing perennial or herbaceous tree",
  "subshrub or climbing shrub",
  "tuberous subshrub",
  "hemiparasitic shrub",
  "hemiepiphytic shrub or tree",
  "annual or shrub",
  "annual, subshrub or shrub"
)

# List of target abbreviations for filtering
#L Liana (to canopy)
#M Medium-sized tree: 10-30 m
#S Shrub: 1-5 m
#T Tree: >30 m
#U Treelet: 5-10 m
  
  
target_codes <- "[LMSTU]"
garwoodlf <- unique(bcilist_fgnames$garwood_lifeform)
garwood_woody_lifeforms <- garwoodlf[grepl(target_codes, garwoodlf) & !grepl("a", garwoodlf, ignore.case = TRUE)]

print(garwood_woody_lifeforms)



bcilist_possibly_woody <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(!woody_forestgeo & is.na(`BCI cult`) &
          (wcvp_accepted_lifeform %in% wcvp_lifeform_woody_list |
           garwood_lifeform %in% garwood_woody_lifeforms 
             )  ) 

datatable(bcilist_possibly_woody %>%
  select(orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, wcvp_accepted_family, 
         wcvp_accepted_lifeform, garwood_lifeform), 
          caption="BCI species that might be woody according to the wcvp lifeform",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

# Save list
write_xlsx(
  list(
    "BCNM possibly woody"   = bcilist_possibly_woody %>% select(all_of(dict_columns$column)),
    "Dictionary" = dict_columns,
    "Metadata"   = metadata_df
  ),
  FILEBCIWOODY
)


```


# Wright revision: Using the BCNM Botanist list to find Wright species that need revision.

Here we check what Wright species are not in the panama woody_forestgeo list but might be woody_forestgeo.

```{r sp4missing}
# Lookup table for woody_forestgeo species
woody_names_lookup <- bind_rows(
  panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "Fcurr") %>% 
    select(match_key = orig_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
  panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "wcvpmatch") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_matched_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
    panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "wcvpaccepted") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
    panwoody_list %>%
    mutate(matchby = "origbinomial") %>%
    select(match_key = orig_binomial, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
  panwoody_list %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpacceptedbinomial") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_binomial, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
  panwoody_synonyms %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "altname") %>% 
    select(match_key = altname, forestgeo_current_name , matchby)
) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  # Group and collapse to keep provenance if a key matched in multiple ways
  group_by(match_key, forestgeo_current_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated) %>%
  summarise(
    matchby = paste(matchby, collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Look up table for BCI species
bcilist_lookup <- bind_rows(
  # 1. Match by Original Name
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "orig_name") %>% 
    select(match_key = orig_name, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
  
  # 2. Match by Original Binomial
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "orig_binomial") %>% 
    select(match_key = orig_binomial, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
  
  # 3. Match by WCVP Matched Name
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpmatch") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_matched_name, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
  
  # 4. Match by WCVP Accepted Name
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpaccepted") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_name, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
  
  # 5. Match by WCVP Accepted Binomial
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpacceptedbinomial") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_binomial, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
    
  # 6. Match by Current Name (from your specific BCI columns)
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "current_name") %>% 
    select(match_key = current_name, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby)
) %>% 
  # Drop NAs and apply your regex cleaning from before
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  # Group and collapse to keep provenance
  group_by(match_key, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo) %>%
  summarise(
    matchby = paste(unique(matchby), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

bcilist_lookup_woody <- bcilist_lookup %>% filter(woody_forestgeo)

wright_woody_match <- allwrighttaxa %>%
  mutate(
    # field if there is a match with the woody_forestgeo list
    panwoody_match = (
      sp6 %in% na.omit(woody_names_lookup$sp6) |
      sp4 %in% na.omit(woody_names_lookup$sp4) |
      orig_name %in% woody_names_lookup$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% woody_names_lookup$match_key |
      wcvp_accepted_name %in% woody_names_lookup$match_key |
      wcvp_matched_name %in% woody_names_lookup$match_key 
    ),
    
    # if there is a match with a species in the BCNM list
    bcnm_match = (
      sp6 %in% na.omit(bcilist_lookup_woody$sp6) |
      sp4 %in% na.omit(bcilist_lookup_woody$sp4) |
      orig_name %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key |
      wcvp_accepted_name %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key |
      wcvp_matched_name %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key 
    ),

    # if there is a match with the woody species in the BCNM list (according to if they are woody in forestgeo)
    bcnm_woody_match = (
      sp6 %in% na.omit(bcilist_lookup_woody$sp6) |
      sp4 %in% na.omit(bcilist_lookup_woody$sp4) |
      orig_name %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key |
      wcvp_accepted_name %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key |
      wcvp_matched_name %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key 
    ),
      
    lifeform_wright_is_woody = if_else(
      is.na(lifeform_wright),
      NA,
      str_detect(lifeform_wright, "TREE|MIDSTORY|UNDERSTORY|SHRUB|LIANA")
    ),
    
    congeners_woody = (word(orig_binomial,1) %in% word(woody_names_lookup$match_key,1)),
    
    climber = case_when(
      is.na(lifeform_wright) ~ NA,
      str_detect(lifeform_wright, "CLIMBER") ~ TRUE,
      str_detect(lifeform_wright, "FREE") ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ NA
    ),
    # check first by s6 code
    lifeform_garwood = bcilist_fgnames$garwood_lifeform[match(allwrighttaxa$sp6,bcilist_fgnames$sp6)],
    # if still NA check by sp4 code
    lifeform_garwood = ifelse(
             is.na(lifeform_garwood),
             bcilist_fgnames$garwood_lifeform[match(allwrighttaxa$sp4,bcilist_fgnames$sp4)],
             lifeform_garwood
            ),
    
    lifeform_garwood_is_woody = if_else(
      is.na(lifeform_garwood),
      NA,
      lifeform_garwood %in% garwood_woody_lifeforms
    ),
        
    lifeform_wcvp_is_woody = if_else(
      is.na(wcvp_lifeform_description),
      NA,
      wcvp_lifeform_description %in% wcvp_lifeform_woody_list
    ),
    
    might_be_woody = case_when(
          lifeform_wright_is_woody %in% TRUE |
          congeners_woody %in% TRUE |
          lifeform_garwood_is_woody %in% TRUE |
          lifeform_wcvp_is_woody %in% TRUE ~ TRUE,
          
          is.na(lifeform_wright_is_woody) &
          is.na(congeners_woody) &
          is.na(lifeform_garwood_is_woody) &
          is.na(lifeform_wcvp_is_woody) ~ NA,
          
          TRUE ~ FALSE
        )
   )


# Table of Wright unmatched species
datatable(wright_woody_match %>% 
            filter(!panwoody_match & Idlevel != "unidentified" &
                     might_be_woody) %>%
            select(sp4, sp6, orig_name,
                   lifeform_wright, lifeform_garwood, wcvp_lifeform_description, bcnm_match
                   ) %>%
            arrange(orig_name),
          caption = "Wright morphospecies missing from the woody_forestgeo list, that might be woody based on the wright lifeform",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


# Table of Wright unmatched species
datatable(wright_woody_match %>% 
            filter(!panwoody_match & Idlevel != "unidentified" &
                     !might_be_woody) %>%
            select(sp4, sp6, orig_name,
                   lifeform_wright, lifeform_garwood, wcvp_lifeform_description, bcnm_match
                   ) %>%
            arrange(orig_name),
          caption = "Wright morphospecies missing from the woody_forestgeo list, that are probably NOT woody based on the wright lifeform",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))
```



```{r savewright}

column_descriptions <- list(
  orig_family = "Family of the current name",
  orig_name               = "Current name in the Wright list",

  sp6                        = "Sp6 code in Wright",
  sp4                        = "Sp4 code in Wright",
  
  Idlevel = "Id level",
  
  
  # WCVP matched fields
  wcvp_matched_name          = "Species name as matched in the World Checklist of Vascular Plants (WCVP)",
  wcvp_matched_status        = "Taxonomic status of the matched name in WCVP (e.g. accepted, synonym)",
  
  # WCVP accepted fields
  wcvp_accepted_name         = "Currently accepted species name according to WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_family       = "Accepted family according to WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_authority    = "Taxonomic authority for the accepted name in WCVP",

  might_be_woody = "TRUE if the lifeform is woody or if it has woody congeners",
  
  climber = "TRUE if the species has climber in the Wright lifeform",
  
  wcvp_lifeform_description     = "Life form classification of the species according to WCVP",

 lifeform_wright = "liferorm according to Wright",
 lifeform_garwood = "Lifeform according to Garwood",
 
 lifeform_wright_is_woody = "True if the Wright lifeform is one of the following: TREE|MIDSTORY|UNDERSTORY|SHRUB|LIANA",
 
 lifeform_garwood_is_woody = paste0("True if the Garwood lifeform is one of the following ", paste(garwood_woody_lifeforms, collapse = ", ")),
                                    
lifeform_wcvp_is_woody = paste0("True if the WCVP lifeform is one of the following: ", paste(wcvp_lifeform_woody_list, collapse=", ")),

congeners_woody = "True if it has congeners in the Current Panama Woody list"
                                    
)

dict_columns <- data.frame(
  column      = names(column_descriptions),
  description = unlist(column_descriptions),
  row.names   = NULL
)

# Dictionary for garwood lifeforms
lifeform_metadata <- data.frame(
  Lifeform_garwood = c("a", "E", "H", "L", "M", "P", "S", "T", "U", "V", "w"),
  Meaning = c("Aquatic", "Epiphyte", "Herb (< 1 m, ; including +- woody subshrubs)", 
              "Liana (to canopy)", "Medium-sized tree: 10-30 m", "Parasite/saprophyte", 
              "Shrub: 1-5 m", "Tree: >30 m", "Treelet: 5-10 m", "Vine (understory)", "Weedy")
)


write_xlsx(
  list(
    "Wright_revision" = wright_woody_match %>%
                          filter(!panwoody_match) %>%
                          select(all_of(dict_columns$column)),
    "Dictionary" = dict_columns,
    "lifeform_garwood" = lifeform_metadata  
  ),
  FILECHECKWRIGHT
)

```

# Panama biota revision
this needs revision...
```{r panamabiotarevision}
pbiota_list$wcvp_accepted_binomial <- paste0(
  pbiota_list$wcvp_accepted_name_genus, " ",
  pbiota_list$wcvp_accepted_name_species
)

# 1. direct name/binomial match
pbiota_garwood_name_match <- pbiota_list %>%
  filter(

    orig_name %in% bcilist_fgnames$orig_name |

    (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
       wcvp_matched_name %in% bcilist_fgnames$wcvp_matched_name) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
       wcvp_accepted_name %in% bcilist_fgnames$wcvp_accepted_name) |

    (!is.na(orig_binomial) & (
        orig_binomial %in% bcilist_fgnames$orig_binomial 
    )) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_binomial) & (
        wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% bcilist_fgnames$wcvp_accepted_binomial 
    )) |
      
    (!is.na(orig_name) & (
        orig_name %in% bcilist_fgnames$current_name 
    ))
  )

# 2. synonym_sp for remaining
pbiota_garwood_synonym_match <- pbiota_list %>%
  filter(
    !orig_name %in% pbiota_garwood_name_match$orig_name &
    (
      orig_name %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_sp |
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
         wcvp_matched_name %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_sp) |
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
         wcvp_accepted_name %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_sp) |
      (!is.na(orig_binomial) & orig_binomial %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_binomial) |
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_binomial) & wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_binomial)
    )
  )

pbiota_garwood_synonym_match$matched_garwood_synonym <- TRUE


# see species that matched by a synonym 
pbiota_garwood_synonym_match_print <- pbiota_garwood_synonym_match %>%  left_join(
    garwood_synonyms %>% select(sp_orig = sp_name, synonym_sp, syn_orig = SynonymTextALL) %>% distinct(),
    by = c("orig_name" = "synonym_sp")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    garwood_synonyms %>% select(sp_match = sp_name, synonym_sp, syn_match = SynonymTextALL) %>% distinct(),
    by = c("wcvp_matched_name" = "synonym_sp"), relationship = "many-to-many"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    garwood_synonyms %>% select(sp_wcvp = sp_name, synonym_sp, syn_wcvp = SynonymTextALL) %>% distinct(),
    by = c("wcvp_accepted_name" = "synonym_sp"),  relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  mutate(
    SynonymMatchAll = coalesce(syn_orig, syn_match, syn_wcvp),
    orig_garwood = coalesce(sp_orig, sp_match, sp_wcvp),
  ) %>%
  select(-syn_orig, -syn_match, -syn_wcvp,  -sp_orig, -sp_match, -sp_wcvp) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, .keep_all = TRUE)
  

datatable(pbiota_garwood_synonym_match_print %>% 
            select(orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, SynonymMatchAll, orig_garwood), 
          caption="Panamabiota species that matched Garwood by synonym",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))
```
